programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve's undercover surveillance operation is hampered by news that his suspect has been deliberately infected with the plague. Dick Van Dyke and Barry Van Dyke star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Sister Michael makes the mistake of employing a man whose troublesome past soon catches up with him - with murderous consequences. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Delta Burke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members have to outthink mentally unstable former member Mark Collins, who has kidnapped Toby and intends to torture and kill him unless he receives details of Walter's research. Robert Patrick, Elyes Gabel and Katharine McPhee star

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Hackers seize control of the US Navy's aircraft and warships, turning their weapons at American cities. Return of the high-octane thriller, starring Elyes Gabriel

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Return of the American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service. Gibbs and McGee travel to Russia to escort a computer expert connected to classified intel. However, the mission is compromised and they are forced to go off the grid while being hunted by Russian mercenaries. Meanwhile, the team in DC frantically tries to locate the missing pair. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy Lieutenant who has been murdered while on his way to a meeting with the President, and must ascertain whether the killing was part of a recent spate of muggings in the area - or a targeted hit to keep information secret. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky works on a case involving his estranged childhood best friend, travelling to London with Special Agent Bishop to interview his pal's family and co-workers. While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members have to outthink mentally unstable former member Mark Collins, who has kidnapped Toby and intends to torture and kill him unless he receives details of Walter's research. Robert Patrick, Elyes Gabel and Katharine McPhee star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. A man called Eugene Keelson contacts Callen and threatens to reveal information about his past unless he runs an errand for him, which involves delivering a flash drive to Bulgarian gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Keelson is found to have had a huge file labelled 'Callen, G', but it is destroyed remotely before Callen can retrieve it. The team is assigned to monitor Iranian terrorists trying to get their hands on a huge hidden fortune that belonged to the former shah, and the case appears to have a concrete link to Callen when a woman in the group's ranks is identified as being his sister Amy. Starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A special agent disappears during an undercover assignment, forcing the team to work alongside the LAPD in a case that involves human-trafficking, money-laundering and drugs. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The officers race against time to track down a highly specialised hit squad that has murdered an agent on assignment in Cyprus, desperate to intervene before a second target is killed. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team tries to find the culprit behind an ambush on a vehicle transporting marines in the desert, which resulted in the death of one man and abduction of two others. Kensi and Deeks bond as they track the SUV that took the missing soldiers, and Sam risks using a compromised cover identity to meet a former contact in a bid to discover if drug cartels were involved. Meanwhile, Nate returns from his mystery assignment

programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. programme.desc: A student reports being attacked in the park on her way home from the bar where she works, but a history of false allegations casts doubt on her story until the Sun Hill watch realise they have a dangerous and increasingly reckless maniac on their hands. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. An 11-year-old boy is abducted by a hooded man in a shopping centre, leading to fears a copycat child killer is on the loose in Sun Hill. Mike Wilson becomes prime suspect when a shrine to convicted murderer Linda Johnson and her deceased partner in crime is found at his home, but as Neil grows closer to his informant in her cell he discovers the case is not nearly as straightforward as it first appeared

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton calls a press conference to tackle the growing media interest in Linda Johnson's involvement in the Clark abduction case, unwittingly attracting the attention of her victims' relatives as well. A ransom note mysteriously appears at the station demanding Linda drop off £10,000 in the park to secure Dean's release, but the ensuing sting operation threatens to end in tragedy

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of an eight-part story. Smithy helps the victim of a knife attack on the Jasmine Allen estate and renews acquaintance with Carly Samuels, a girl he had been helping, who witnessed the incident. It soon emerges that Carly is afraid of her violent boyfriend and keen to start a new life miles away from London. Meanwhile, Beth and Nate encounter a young carer who seeks relief from his responsibilities by setting fire to cars

programme.title: Bramwell

programme.desc: Gillian Kearney stars in the feature-length period drama following the fortunes of housekeeper Emily Kennedy and her relationships with three very different men. At first she is content working for the McGilby family while her father is away at sea, but finds her life thrown into turmoil by the death of one of her employers. With John Bowler and Ray Stevenson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar readies itself for Christmas 1960 with a festive bus trip to see the Regent Street lights, while a visit from a BBC film crew promises to make it a festive holiday to remember. However, it's a difficult time for grieving mother Iris, bringing back memories of her long-departed baby daughter. At Nonnatus, things are tense as Sister Monica Joan determines to bring the Christmas spirit early - despite Sister Evangelina's disgust. As the community prepares for a televised carol concert, it becomes apparent that Sister Monica Joan has gone missing. Jenny Agutter and Judy Parfitt star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Easter approaches in the spring of 1961, and Patsy helps to deliver a baby that is born with severe deformities. Her husband struggles to cope with his daughter's needs, but the sisters rally round to support the family. Trixie teaches a keep fit class which results in a medical emergency, leaving her determined to teach her patients to understand their own bodies

programme.title: Death in Holy Orders
programme.desc: Conclusion. The death count at St Anselm's theological college continues to mount, leaving the school threatened with closure. Thriller based on PD James's book, starring Martin Shaw, with Robert Hardy, Janie Dee and Jesse Spencer

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A head teacher confronts the dealers preying on his community as the team deal with the consequences of a surge in drug-related crime. With Colin Salmon

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh struggles to prove his innocence and win back Penny after failing a drug test. Meryl and Charlie lock horns over Hayley's entry in the Miss Whyhope competition

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh and Penny desperately seek a kidney donor to save Jim's life, Charlie is pursued by an obsessive fan, while Matt and Ajax track down a thief with eyes on their farm

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of eight. Smithy battles in vain to save Carly's life following the shooting and struggles with his feelings back at the station when he is tasked as the family liaison officer. A mobile unit is set up on the estate to help with community problems in light of the gang violence, and Beth's decision to bring in Harry the dog proves a winner with Nate when he realises how popular he is with the girls. Cleveland Campbell guest stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Frieda is annoyed by Penny's sycophantic attitude toward a lazy and arrogant locum working on the AAU, and later surprises her colleague by performing a procedure only a trained doctor could normally carry out. Meanwhile, Faye receives the results of the Lowe Syndrome test on her unborn baby, and Lady Byrne reveals she has big plans for the child. Guest starring Jane Asher

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The racist thugs return to the hospital after one is injured during an attack on a gay soldier, and Guppy finds himself surrounded by the gang when they spot him in the car park. Abs sees a fracas develop as he walks past, but decides not to get involved. Woody wants to report Josh for hitting an unruly child, and Jas rebuffs his son once again when he makes an effort to break the ice

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Third of an eight-part story. Diane and Nate investigate an attack on a doctor on the Jasmine Allen estate and discover his colleague Dr Rachel Cartwright (Josette Simon) has been performing illegal operations. Elsewhere, Carly Samuels' grieving mother Leanne is upset by a psychic's claim that she 'saw' Tito Morientes get into the car used in the drive-by shooting, but nobody is brave enough to give evidence leading to a prosecution

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy has second thoughts about the hit man, and David quizzes her. Grant and Tiffany go for a baby scan

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian's been shot and is close to unconsciousness, but will Cindy help him - or leave him to die?

As Marsha's troubles deepen, she finds solace in a bottle and spurns all offers of help from her friends, while Williams and Phillips' relationship reaches a turning point

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A farmer faces the loss of his savings as his livestock falls victim to a seemingly incurable disease, and James is powerless to help, while Tristan returns from school and admits to James that his exams may not have gone all that well

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A property developer approaches Lady Jane's husband and offers him a business partnership, making Lovejoy suspicious. Starring Ian McShane, with guest appearances by Nicky Henson and Sherrie Hewson

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Karl Howman stars as charming painter who loves life - and ladies! Lesley finds a new job, Veronica has a blind date, and Jacko gets a nasty shock

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Miss Brahms becomes the store spokesperson when Grace Brothers tries out closed-circuit advertising. Comedy, with Wendy Richard, Mollie Sugden and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg and Seymour visit an old friend in hospital following his hernia operation and go to extraordinary lengths to cheer him up. Peter Sallis and Michael Aldridge star in the first episode to feature Clem 'Smiler' Hemmingway (Stephen Lewis)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour sets about proving that skiing does not have to be an expensive sport. However, with snow in short supply he determines that he, Compo and Clegg need to use dinner trays. Long-running rural comedy, starring Michael Aldridge, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A woman's body is found on the moors, and although it looks like suicide, Gently is convinced it is suspicious - even after finding out the victim had mood swings and suffered from motor neurone disease. She was a GP, estranged from her husband, and it isn't long before he is in the frame, although as the investigation moves to the doctor's home community - the same place where Rachel grew up - a secretive local company provides a new lead. Meanwhile, Bacchus celebrates his promotion, leaving Gently needing a new sergeant. Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and Lisa McGrillis star, with Lesley Nicol (Downton Abbey) and Jason Done (Waterloo Road)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Brian spends time at home making peace with himself over his dismissal, he agrees to help Esther's friend Margaret find her missing brother Peter, in the interest of keeping his mind active. Peter was the accountant of a man who has gone to prison for the murder of his wife, so Brian visits the convict hoping he will be able to assist. However, his investigation begins to encroach on the Ucos team's efforts to track down a conman who may be connected to several outstanding cases. With Rosalind Ayres and Art Malik

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team attends a triple murder at a farmhouse, where the investigating detective, Supt Tom Byrne, wastes no time making his presence felt. The pathologists soon make a breakthrough when DNA found at the scene matches that of a mysterious female serial killer - although Nikki has cause to question the direction they are heading in. The case is proving difficult for Leo, however, whose mind is in turmoil following the suicide of an old friend. Guest starring Vincent Regan and Shelley Conn

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh creates havoc for Charlie when he reveals her secret talent. Penny's first day back in Whyhope is headed for disaster until she joins forces with Hugh

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: The staff unite to save one of their own. A former slave turned activist arrives in town, causing a rift between Foster and Mary. Guest starring Bad Girls' Victoria Alcock (Julie Saunders)

programme.desc: Fourth of an eight-part story. Carly's funeral descends into chaos as the crowd chant 'murderer' at chief suspect Tito Morientes and a gunshot shatters the glass of the hearse. programme.desc: Kathy asks for Grant's help in her search for the truth. Cindy is under increasing pressure - will David come to her rescue?

programme.desc: Colette is overjoyed to learn that the Fusiliers will shortly be leaving for South Africa, and tries to persuade Joe that it would be the ideal place to get married. Marsha learns that Michael has been having an affair and resolves to fight for him, no matter what it takes

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Angus Grier's moment of triumph proves short-lived as James stumbles on his guilty secret, while Tristan faces up to telling Siegfried about his exam results, and thinks a lifestyle change might help get his brother back on side

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Part one of two. An American acquaintance of Lovejoy's is sold some fake antiques, so he sets out to exact revenge on the dealer responsible - and make a little money into the bargain. Linda Gray and Brian Blessed guest star, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: British sitcom about the misadventures of a cockney painter and decorator. Jacko finds himself in a desperate predicament - but can he get out of trouble?

programme.title: Are You Being Served?

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When the Teesdales stop to give Clegg, Compo and Seymour a lift home from their caravanning holiday, Mrs T tries to fight off a feeling of discomfort as it becomes apparent the trio are determined to help park the caravan in their drive. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of an Asian man's body sparks another case for Lynley and Havers, plunging them into a murky world of illicit sexuality, racial tension and family loyalties. As the investigation becomes increasingly tangled and complex, Havers takes drastic action to save a child's life - which leaves her career in jeopardy. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small, with Saeed Jaffrey

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of pornographer Jim Hockney is reopened when his daughter Sara provides DNA proof he wasn't her father. The team tries to track down jazz singer Angela Gold, who was the victim's girlfriend at the time and disappeared from the music scene after being acquitted of the crime, and it's Brian's replacement Danny Griffin (Nicholas Lyndhurst) who manages to find her. However, with Angela professing her innocence and Sara's mother Jane also arousing suspicion, it becomes a struggle to determine who is telling the truth. With a guest appearance by former Corrie killer Brian Capron

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki sees a barefoot girl being knocked over by a car, marking the beginning of a case that takes the team into the harrowing world of sex grooming and teenage prostitution. The police know the girl has been abused and suspicion soon falls on her stepfather. Meanwhile, an early-morning bath saves Harry's life, and two puzzling corpses are brought in to the centre - a heavily tattooed man and a decomposed body found at the airport. Guest starring Sanjeev Bhaskar, Elyes Gabel and Madeline Duggan

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Southampton v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Premier League encounter at St Mary's Stadium. Southampton are looking to complete a double over the Seagulls, having come back from behind to prevail 2-1 when the sides met at Amex Stadium in December thanks to goals by Jannik Vestergaard and Danny Ings, but Brighton have generally been slightly stronger on their travels this term. With analysis from Dion Dublin

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Ireland recorded a 19-12 victory when the sides met at Aviva Stadium in last season's competition, with Jonathan Sexton scoring the only try of the game kicking his side's other 14 points, and they also prevailed 22-13 here in 2019, but the Scots have produced some strong recent performances and will be hopeful of revenge. Gabby Logan presents, with analysis from John Barclay, Martin Johnson and Andy Nicol, and commentary from Eddie Butler, Chris Paterson and Jamie Heaslip

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison joins Adam Henson and his team for 48 hours of hectic lambing. She may be the rookie in Adam's lambing shed, but Ellie quickly gets to grips with multiple births, touch-and-go adoptions, and the emotions of the life-or-death decisions faced every day during this busiest of spring seasons on the farm. Tom Heap is on the case to find out why mountains of wool, historically the nation's most valuable sheep product, are now piling up in warehouses across the country

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in London. Treasures include a pair of hands crafted by Lucian Freud, a destination board from the front of an early London bus, and a model of Donald Campbell's Bluebird, which broke the land-speed record in 1964. There is also a rare set of wedding jewellery from Nepal, and a diamond cross and gold necklace with a hefty price tag

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Tom Brannick enlists the help of Heather Pentland to find out what Jackie Twomey knows about Goliath. Tori Matthews and Tom's daughter Izzy put pressure on Tom to get to the truth. With lives on the line, Tom must unmask Goliath before it's too late. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v West Ham United at Old Trafford. Spurs prevailed 2-0 over their North London rivals in December's reverse fixture thanks to goals by the deadly duo of Heung-Min Son and Harry Kane, but the Gunners will have been determined for revenge on home soil. United had already defeated the Hammers at home in the FA Cup and also prevailed 3-1 in the league at London Stadium. Plus, Leicester City v Sheffield United and Southampton v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Married couple Sam and Louise from Aberdeen take on The Wall, hoping to combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Will they get the questions right? And can they walk away with cash to buy a bigger house for when they can start a family? Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a renovation development in Gillingham, a house in north Chingford and a semi in Wolverhampton

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers raid a Birmingham sweatshop suspected of being a major supplier of counterfeit clothing, and pursue a builder who rendered a family home unfit to be lived in

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help teams at the East of England Showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about a collection of fountain pens

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca's past creates trouble for him when Jonno tells all to Zara. Bear is alarmed to realise a care service for a teenage boy is just a front for something more sinister

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah meets a couple from Warwickshire who have a dream of owning a property in north-west Wales that would provide them with a home and the ability to offer holiday lets

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Jeremy Pang and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by friends Paul and Roberto, who have high-pressure jobs and like to barbecue together to relax

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Will Kirk takes on the huge responsibility of restoring a model ship, carefully handcrafted out of matchsticks by a beloved father who died when his children were young

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As the government faces mounting criticism that well-connected people made millions out of Britain's PPE crisis, Panorama investigates who won out. More than £12billion was spent in the first six months of the pandemic on contracts to provide personal protective equipment. Reporter Richard Bilton meets one man who made £40million on a deal, and speaks to others who felt ignored in favour of less experienced suppliers. As the government refuses to reveal the full details of all its so-called VIP deals, the programme reveals the high-profile connections to one lucrative contract

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea is nervous as the smuggling job looms, and to make things worse Caleb arrives with an extra package to carry, once again threatening her to make sure all goes to plan. Kathy receives an unexpected phone call, Ash's likes catch up with her and Bailey is heartbroken as the Taylors say a tough goodbye. Over at the Vic, Sharon has found a new place to live as Mick and Linda's flat has come back onto the market

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: In the first episode of the classic sitcom, beleaguered and badgered hotelier Basil Fawlty tries to attract a better class of clientele, but ends up being taken to the cleaners by the first seemingly upper-crust customer to check in. Starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales, Andrew Sachs and Connie Booth

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the latest heat, as five more hopefuls get their first chance to win an apron by cooking their Signature Dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. After tasting all five plates, John and Gregg pick their favourite dishes, and those cooks will earn themselves not only a MasterChef apron but also a place in the next round

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' acting skills are put to the test, with EastEnders' Natalie Cassidy giving them a masterclass in the art. Michelle Visage and Graham Norton are on the judging panel, alongside an extra special guest judge

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by comic actress Amy Poehler, star and director of the rebellious new comedy Moxie and comedian Rob Beckett, who is promoting his podcast Lockdown Parenting Hell. Call the Midwife's Leonie Elliott also guests, alongside Hollywood star Jennifer Garner and singer-songwriter Nick Jonas. With music from singer-songwriter Tom Odell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Martin Roberts look around properties in Blackpool, Derby and the West Midlands town of Bilston, before hearing how they have been redeveloped

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers keep an eye out for coach drivers who refuse to take a break despite being exhausted, and track down two fraudsters running a sim swap scam

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help two teams buy items at Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits the Museum of Timekeeping in Newark

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel tries his best to end the hunger strike once and for all. A young woman in Ruhma's care is warned not to eat during labour but her overbearing father has other ideas

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a dog groomer who wants to relocate her business to Suffolk on a budget of £320,000. He also meets the team behind an ambitious project to restore an Anglo-Saxon longship, the remains of which were unearthed by an amateur archaeologist in the 1930s

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Hertfordshire friends Adam and Dan both follow a vegan diet, so today's ingredients for Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang include everything from vegan staples to more exotic produce. They both hope to walk away with some great new recipes - but who will leave as the winner? Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the culinary contest

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy restorers Amanda and Julie revive a sad dog that has been badly burned in a devastating house fire, metal expert Dominic tries to get a rusty retro kitchen mixer beating again and silversmith Brenton works his magic on a nurse's belt buckle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon asks Kathy how Gavin died and is shocked when she realises Kathy is planning to go to the memorial service - as far as she is concerned, he may have been her biological father but he can rot in hell. Peter finds himself caught in the middle when Ash and Suki argue, and Bailey finds a stray dog - but Karen says they cannot afford to keep it

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki tells Ash she should do anything necessary to get her job back, so Peter assures her he will say he gave himself the medication. Mitch explains that Bailey is heartbroken over losing Bronson so Rainie enlists Stuart's help to find the stray dog. As an emotional Sharon packs up her things up in the Vic, Kim tries to organise her a leaving party

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira tells Hanssen that he may not have been Reyhan's only victim, leading the great surgeon to become distracted while operating on her father. Chloe realises Ange is missing and grows increasingly suspicious that Cameron has something to do with her disappearance. Louis gets into an altercation with a former friend who has turned up seeking revenge

programme.title: Roman Kemp: Our Silent Emergency
programme.desc: Last August, Roman Kemp's life changed dramatically with the sudden and unexpected death of his best friend, radio producer Joe Lyons. It led him to question why increasing numbers of young men are taking their own lives and why so many of them never ask for help. Here, Roman sets out to explore what can be done to encourage people to seek help, what preventative action others need to be taking, and the lasting impact of mental health and suicide on friends and family

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Sheriff Romero responds to Norma's 911 call and has a surprising proposal for her concerning the deaths of Keith Summers and Zack Shelby. Even with those recent events now behind them, Norma reacts badly to Dylan's decision to move into his own place. The hotel is not quite open yet but gets its first guest Jake Abernathy, who claims to have been a regular when Summers owned the place

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is upset after Emma tells some of the other girls that he and Bradley slept together. He leaves school without permission, and in a meeting with the principal and his teacher it is suggested to Norma that Norman should seek professional help. The session with the psychologist does not go well as the patient shows signs of severe strain

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain hurdling great Colin Jackson, former England striker Darren Bent, ex-WBA Super Middleweight champion boxer George Groves and Commonwealth Games gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the multicultural community of Wednesfield, Wolverhampton, who are hoping to go one better than the silver award they won last year. But they do not have a plan, have a shoestring budget and are relying on locals to donate the plants

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden's balloon travels over the flattest part of Britain as she descends on Norfolk. She helps cut a hole in a hedge at East Ruston Gardens, assists in preparing a tree for felling at Bressingham Gardens, and discovers the human history of the Norfolk Broads

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith visits the Colne Valley Regional Park, a landscape of forest, fields and waterways on the edge of west London. She does battle with an invasive species, gets up close with tiny creatures that show the health of the park's rivers, meets a savvy septuagenarian saving her farm from development, and helps secure a future for one of our most endangered mammals, the water vole. Tom Heap asks whether eco-education should be at the heart of our school system, Adam Henson introduces new boar George II to his sows, and wildlife cameraman Jack Perks gets up close with otters

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at more floral highlights from 2020, including alliums and auriculas, houseplants and creative containers, as well as the scented stars of the summer garden. There's also another chance to see what the Gardeners' World viewers were getting up to in their gardens last year as they share their horticultural adventures

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway is in fine voice when he kicks off his walk in Cardigan, home of the first Eisteddfod. After visiting the castle, lovingly restored by locals, he learns about the dying 'art' of Seine net fishing before climbing the famous mount at Mwnt. He also gets to feed an alpaca and take tea with the Women's Institute at his final destination, Aberporth - where he meets a fellow meteorologist, 98-year-old Joan

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from the programme

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef begins an exploration of the country's food in the states of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, where the influence of the British Empire on eating habits can still be observed. Rick enjoys the prominence of fish and seafood in the cuisine found in the cities of Kolkata and Chennai, cooking dishes including prawns in coconut milk and his mum's British Raj curry

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Gary Pe help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales, while host Charlie Ross shares his passion for cricket

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Brenda Edwards finds out how St Elizabeth's in Stockport makes Mothering Sunday special, and there is a visit to a new Christian community grocery run by a Manchester-based charity

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of film and TV appearances made by the actor, dancer and choreographer, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Singin' in the Rain (1952)
programme.desc: A silent-movie star tries to win the heart of an aspiring actress as the introduction of sound changes the film business for ever. However, his frequent on-screen love interest does not take kindly to this real-life romance. Musical comedy, starring Gene Kelly, Debbie Reynolds, Donald O'Connor, Jean Hagen and Millard Mitchell. Songs include Make 'em Laugh, You Are My Lucky Star and the title number

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Raj Bisram value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, and Paul Martin finds out how the myth of Merlin has been connected to the heart of Wales

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Cairngorms, hill farmer Johnny and his son gather in their sheep for pregnancy scanning and mineral dosing on a cold snowy day. Meanwhile in County Down, Northern Ireland, the Rodgers family's cattle are tested by a vet for Bovine TB, and unfortunately find out that six animals are found to be infected, and have to be sent away to slaughter

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris Packham shines a light on some of the species that show how brainpower, ingenuity and clever tactics are just as important as brawn in the daily battle for survival. Spotted hyenas play politics to gain power, and the female with the most friends rules their group, while being a team player can help when it comes to getting a meal - as demonstrated by vampire bats, who share mouthfuls of blood with strangers in the hope of building a bond and receiving the favour in return

programme.title: Brazil with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The seasoned traveller explores the South American country, beginning in the north-east - where Europeans first landed and grew rich on the profits from sugar and tobacco plantations run with slave labour. In Sao Luis, Michael finds out about a ceremony based on a 200-year-old tale before heading to the coastal lagoons of the Lencois Maranhenses National Park. Journeying inland, he gets a glimpse of the fast-disappearing world of old-style cowboys known as vaqueiros, has his fortune read by a Candomble priest and learns to drum with the Olodum cultural collective

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined on the topical comedy show by guests Richard Madeley and Maisie Adam, as well as his mum and his hand-picked focus group of 20. Together they take a humorous look at the week's biggest talking points, from front page news to trending topics, helping the nation to navigate their way through these tricky times

programme.title: Beep
programme.desc: Tommy is lying in his hospital bed in a coma and on his wedding anniversary he is visited by his wife Liz and various other members of his dysfunctional family, including his emotionally challenged son Martin and sleep-deprived daughter Hannah. Comedy, starring Siobhan Redmond and Paul Higgins

programme.title: Stephen Merchant: Hello Ladies
programme.desc: The Office co-creator's critically acclaimed 2011 stand-up comedy show, based on his experiences of the rocky road to finding love - a quest in which being taller-than-average doesn't always help

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: A collection of distinctive comedy shorts from the next generation, showcasing creative talent from all across the UK and beyond

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claire shares her secret with Gretchen and invites her to be room-mates. Sylar continues to cause chaos in Matt's mind while he and his partner seek evidence against a drug lord. Samuel travels to New York, where he tricks his way into Peter's life, and a deaf filing clerk comes to terms with her new-found abilities. Fantasy drama, starring Jack Coleman, Hayden Panettiere and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: While Hiro is trying to stop a colleague from committing suicide, Ando persuades him to tell his sister that he is terminally ill. Tracy struggles to control her powers, and finds she is no longer able to deal with the indignities of her former job. Meanwhile, Nathan's mother helps him regain some of his memories. Drama, starring Masi Oka, Ali Larter and Adrian Pasdar

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Simon Parfett and his team meet Tammy and Colin. Wanting to spend more time together as a family and create extra income, they decided to buy a horsebox which they could rent out to festival-goers. Alas, things didn't go to plan, so Simon and company come to the rescue

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, as they hunt for potentially profitable items to take to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also looks at the art and history of Persian carpets

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where items assessed include a rare artefact that survived the attack on Pearl Harbour and an LS Lowry painting. There are also ancient carvings from the Indian subcontinent and an intriguing wooden carving with a mysterious past

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Creativity week begins with the launch of The Big Song

programme.title: Great Expectations
programme.desc: Ray Winstone and Douglas Booth star in this three-part dramatisation of the classic Dickens novel about an orphan's rise through high society with the assistance of a mysterious benefactor. Eleven-year-old Pip's life is changed dramatically by an encounter with escaped convict Abel Magwitch. With David Suchet, Gillian Anderson, Mark Addy and Claire Rushbrook

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian takes on Eric Knowles at a car boot sale in Essex, where questionable bric-a-brac and bad weather are the order of the day

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian face off at an auction in Somerset, where Eric spots a sleeper in a job lot of china

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Properties in St Albans, Hertfordshire, are judged by neighbours, beginning with a 1930s space-saving conversion, a Victorian terrace, and a traditional two-up, two-down

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A compilation edition, featuring finds at Bletchley Park, the Ulster Transport Museum and Crowcombe Court. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers a fascinating family history in Cornwall

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team travel to Wiltshire to meet Anthony and Bianca who have grand plans to turn the shed at the bottom of their garden into a unique B&B

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary opens her garden to host a village fete and shares her flair for turning easy dishes into spectacular crowd-pleasers, and she is visited by two brothers for a lesson in the art of making authentic Italian pizzas

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Kimi Raikkonen, the county of Gloucestershire, Agatha Christie's Poirot and lidos in Britain. before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the second semi-final of the quiz as two teams compete to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance, with one set of clues asking what completes the sequence of The Iliad, The Aeneid and The Lord of the Rings (technically)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-finals conclude, with two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win a second match and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade along the way. This time, Giles and Monica help out at the Torridon in the Highlands, Britain's most northerly five-star hotel. Its need to balance a luxury stay with being self-sufficient has Monica collecting seaweed for the kitchen and helping to herd Highland cattle, while Giles learns to change toilet seats and joins the staff in their afternoon off - for a swim in the beautiful but freezing-cold loch

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from December 2019, with actor Brian Cox and comedian and actress Roisin Conaty joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Dunblane: Our Story
programme.desc: Documentary about the mass shooting in Dunblane in 1996, when Thomas Hamilton killed 16 pupils and their teacher at a primary school. The film features testimony from a survivor who was five at the time and details her terrifying experience, and also includes an interview with the then-headmaster of the school, as well as contributions from family members of the survivors and the dead, including two women who talk about sisters they never knew

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty travels across the country to understand what race and racism mean in the UK, in the wake of the international conversation triggered by the killing of George Floyd

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is suspended, unable to explain why one of her patients died - and as she leaves she confides her suspicion to Ange that Cameron was involved in the patient's death. Sahira considers bumping her father up the transplant list and on Lucky's last day she pulls out all the stops to help a patient with a phobia of hospitals. Will it be enough for Max to keep the psychiatric nurse on at Holby?

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith visits the Colne Valley Regional Park, a landscape of forest, fields and waterways on the edge of west London. She does battle with an invasive species, gets up close with tiny creatures that show the health of the park's rivers, meets a savvy septuagenarian saving her farm from development, and helps secure a future for one of our most endangered mammals, the water vole. Tom Heap asks whether eco-education should be at the heart of our school system, Adam Henson introduces new boar George II to his sows, and wildlife cameraman Jack Perks gets up close with otters

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help teams at the East of England Showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about a collection of fountain pens

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Jeremy Pang and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by friends Paul and Roberto, who have high-pressure jobs and like to barbecue together to relax

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Designers Manoj Malde and Tom Massey come up with alternative ideas for the West Sussex garden of wheelchair user Tracey, who can only venture into certain parts of her current space with assistance, and her husband Jake, who dreams of having a low-maintenance plot that he doesn't have to keep looking neat and tidy. Plus, Joel Bird helps a Liverpool couple turn their tiny, dull courtyard into a colourful paradise. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Music takes centre stage today as popstar Gracey talks about songwriting

programme.title: Great Expectations
programme.desc: Herbert Pocket takes Pip under his wing and tries to turn him into a gentleman, but the youngster's expensive taste sees him racking up debts, and his spending spirals farther out of control when he meets the charming and confident baronet Bentley Drummle. Miss Havisham asks Pip to visit her at Satis House, keen to see his transformation first-hand. With Douglas Booth, Harry Lloyd and Gillian Anderson

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian visit Sint-Truiden in Belgium, where Danny finds an antique sewing machine and Eric finds out how modern bronzes are cast

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian meet for the final competition of the week, the showdown. With half their items going to auction, it remains to be seen whether they will be able to turn a profit at all. Eric gets passionate about pottery when he finds a Davenport dish and Danny risks it all at the auction

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: A family home with an industrial twist, a sleek, split-level semi-detached property, and an eclectic Edwardian abode with a menagerie of surprises are all judged by neighbours

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, and also visits a family business that builds racing cars. With experts Mark Stacey and Caroline Hawley

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: A trip to Devon sees Simon Parfett and his team help Lucy and Rob, who are setting up their perfect B&B overlooking the Teign Valley in a '70s Californian airstream

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: The bikers head for Finland hoping for a change of pace from the intensity of St Petersburg, and they immediately fall in love with the tranquil and beautiful lakes. Dave learns how to enjoy a traditional Finnish sauna experience, while Si cooks up some Finnish pastries, before things take a more hectic turn as they ride tanks through the forest, learn about the national pastime of wife-carrying and meet a priest who has given his service a heavy metal twist

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As semi-final week kicks off, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two restaurants in Hebden Bridge. Judge Michelle Ogundehin reveals that the competitors will be working in pairs, with each designer responsible for a single floor in the restaurant. Who will make it to the final?

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, triplets Peter, David and Phillip, who were adopted as young boys, have their DNA analysed by Professor Turi King as they attempt to explore their birth father's heritage. Meanwhile, a woman who was conceived from a sperm donor longs to learn about the other half of her ancestry, and find out if she has any half donor siblings

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined on the topical comedy show by guests Richard Madeley and Maisie Adam, as well as his mum and his hand-picked focus group of 20. Together they take a humorous look at the week's biggest talking points, from front page news to trending topics, helping the nation to navigate their way through these tricky times

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris Packham shines a light on some of the species that show how brainpower, ingenuity and clever tactics are just as important as brawn in the daily battle for survival. Spotted hyenas play politics to gain power, and the female with the most friends rules their group, while being a team player can help when it comes to getting a meal - as demonstrated by vampire bats, who share mouthfuls of blood with strangers in the hope of building a bond and receiving the favour in return

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: In a special programme to end this series, Mary is in conversation with British writer Jed Mercurio. He's the mastermind behind hit show Line of Duty, returning for a new series in the spring, and has a rich back catalogue of attention-grabbing TV drama including Bodyguard, Cardiac Arrest and Bodies. How much does his early medical and military training inform his writing? And how much drama does it take to make real events into unmissable television?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return with the cookery competition as 40 contestants are put through gruelling culinary challenges to test not just their skills, but also their knowledge of food and ability to cope in a professional kitchen. They have to cook not only for their place in the competition but also for the right to wear a coveted MasterChef apron, with the first five contestants beginning by preparing their signature dish

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences

programme.title: The Plank (1967)
programme.desc: Two clumsy workmen have the job of transporting a plank through a London suburb to a building site. However, this simple task proves far from straightforward, as they cause a series of bizarre mishaps and accidents along the way. Slapstick comedy directed by Eric Sykes, who also stars alongside Tommy Cooper

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The Liverpool-born actress reminisces about her career, including her successes with director and ex-husband Mike Leigh, with whom she created the monstrous Beverly in 1970s play Abigail's Party. She focuses on her early screen appearances, and reveals what influenced her choice of roles. With clips from Gavin & Stacey, Newshounds, Nuts in May and Frost Weekly

programme.title: Twin Sisters: A World Apart
programme.desc: The story of identical twin girls Alexandra and Mia, who were adopted separately from China, with one growing up in California, and the other in Sweden. A chance sighting at the orphanage prompted the adoptive parents to suspect the girls were related, and a DNA test proved their theory. The film follows the children as they meet in Norway aged eight years old, and captures the similarities between the pair, despite having grown up thousands of miles apart

programme.title: I Got Life! (2017)
programme.desc: A woman, separated from her husband, loses her job and then finds out she is going to be a grandmother. However, her life takes an unexpected turn when she crosses paths with the great love of her youth. Drama, starring Agnes Jaoui and Thibault de Montalembert. In French

programme.title: Opera Mums with Bryony Kimmings
programme.desc: Inspired by the lives of single mums and informed by opera experts at the English National Opera, performance artist Bryony Kimmings writes and directs her own operetta. The demands of the opera industry are turned on their head by Kimmings' insistence that real life is just as deserving of high art as the stories of the rich and powerful

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The Liverpool-born actress reminisces about her career, including her successes with director and ex-husband Mike Leigh, with whom she created the monstrous Beverly in 1970s play Abigail's Party. She focuses on her early screen appearances, and reveals what influenced her choice of roles. With clips from Gavin & Stacey, Newshounds, Nuts in May and Frost Weekly

programme.title: Twin Sisters: A World Apart
programme.desc: The story of identical twin girls Alexandra and Mia, who were adopted separately from China, with one growing up in California, and the other in Sweden. A chance sighting at the orphanage prompted the adoptive parents to suspect the girls were related, and a DNA test proved their theory. The film follows the children as they meet in Norway aged eight years old, and captures the similarities between the pair, despite having grown up thousands of miles apart

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of humans and animals over the course of a year in Alaska, which is by far the biggest US state and still one of the wildest places on Earth with deep forests and vast mountain ranges. The arrival of spring sees Alaska go through a huge transformation as water, light and warmth return, with animals emerging from hibernation and rushing to cash in on the riches of the season. Dougray Scott narrates

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best sets out to uncover and explore some of Northern Ireland's lost railways, beginning with a visit to Co Down's answer to Stonehenge. The presenter also hears about a battle to control Newcastle's growth as a tourist resort, as well as enjoying a day at the races

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates an unexpected place of respite for the weary traveller by painting a lonesome cabin in the middle of a snowy setting

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the history of two pictures, both of which are believed to be by Paul Gauguin, one of the giants of 19th-century art

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens reveals how, as the 20th century dawned, artists found radical new ways to depict Wales, pushing themselves to extremes and redefining what art could be. His venture takes him to the National Museum Wales in Cardiff, Bardsey Island off the Llyn Peninsula, Colwyn Bay and Swansea

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of the child abuse scandal that erupted on the island of Jersey in 2007. The victims' voices had remained unheard for years, but when widespread allegations of sexual abuse resurfaced in the late 2000s, Jersey's then-health minister Stuart Syvret spoke out about the scale of this historic child abuse and the damage done to the victims. Syvret's words sparked a moment of reckoning for the small community, whose leaders were determined to protect the island's reputation, home to a trillion dollars in offshore investment. As bitterness and enmity dragged on and the press deserted Jersey, two unlikely bloggers decided to reignite the unresolved issues

programme.title: Paula Rego: Secrets and Stories
programme.desc: Making use of new interviews and a vast archive of home video, film-maker Nick Willing compiles an intimate portrait of his mother, the celebrated painter Paula Rego. While Rego has been notoriously private and guarded about her personal life, she opens up to her son, discussing secrets and stories from her live, which has seen her battle fascism, misogyny in art world, and manic depression. Willing's film dictates his mother's story from her native Portugal, where she used her pictures as a weapon against dictatorship, through to settling in London, where she turned her craft toward tackling women's issues such as abortion rights

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: Walk the Line

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a landscape featuring all his trademarks - a lake, tall trees and a cabin with a majestic mountain in the background

programme.desc: Hacker is challenged to convince Sir Humphrey that a computer holding vital citizen information could raise concerns about people's rights to privacy. Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne star

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Elizabeth has to stay with her ailing mother and tells Reggie to cancel a dinner party they have arranged - but he comes up with a better idea. Leonard Rossiter, Pauline Yates and John Barron star

programme.title: Ireland's Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the Irish artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others

programme.title: Crock of Gold: A Few Rounds with Shane MacGowan (2020)
programme.desc: Director Julien Temple explores the wild life of beloved punk poet Shane MacGowan, the frontman of popular Anglo-Irish Celtic punk band the Pogues, who found fame in the 1980s and 1990s. The film combines interviews, archive footage and animations as it charts the singer's early days in Ireland, his time on the streets of London, where he embraced the punk scene, and his time with the Pogues and beyond. At the heart of the film is a new interview with MacGowan, revealing his passions, humour and deep knowledge of music, history, spirituality and popular culture

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Believing that Kalle is in danger, Mikko traces his phone and rushes to a neighbouring island where he finds and accosts Leo. Kalle, who has overheard his uncle's confession, tells Mikko who really killed Tommi. In Finnish

programme.desc: Ellu admits to Tytti that he doesn't think that her mother likes him as Daniel drives Anna to their surprise weekend away. In Finnish

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: In the last episode of the series, Mimi enlists her brother Leo’s help to fulfil a lifelong dream: to win a Blue Peter badge. She teaches Leo to rollerblade, they upcycle a mirror together and draw portraits of each other, only to discover they are too old to apply for a badge! We meet proud badge holders Iris and Aidan, and find out all about the Chinese Mid- Autumn Festival, which they celebrate with their mum and dad. Luckily for Mimi, Blue Peter’s very own Mwaksy invites Mimi to co-host Fan Club Live, and there is a special surprise for her in the post.

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield hosts as two teams fight it out to dodge detention and put the cool back into school. In this episode, Mr Learner is training to be a music teacher and is auditioning everyone for the school choir, managing to bring the house down! Trying to win Lauren’s shiny gold stars are team captains Olivia and Nayan. They are joined by Blue Peter’s Lyndsey Russell and actress Dúaa Karim plus comedians Sam Fletcher and Helen Bauer. They all battle it out in a series of hilarious rounds guaranteed to make you laugh! Rounds include Say What and Stick to the Point, the word association game where teams are put on the spot by Lauren and her stick of pointiness, and everyone’s favourite, Pie the Supply. This week they are all pretending to be home economics teachers, but only one of them is telling the truth. So watch someone get pied in the face before the final round, Gnomework, where teams must dash across gunge infested fields to grab some homework to avoid getting detention.

Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse's over-reliance on gadgets prompts Colonel K to send his top agent on a tech-free nature retreat. Unbeknownst to Danger Mouse, Greenback has the same plans for his daughter, Delilah, whose social media obsession has also spiralled out of control. Back at HQ Penfold is tasked with protecting the entire world from the threat of super-villainy. Stripped off his gadgets, DM struggles to adapt to life in the wilderness and, when a talking pinecone called Conni begins to offer him survival tips, he begins to question his own sanity. programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. programme.desc: Lola's family are from Spain, a country in southern Europe. She is making a traditional Spanish dish called empanadas for her and her friends. Lola begins by rolling out two pieces of pizza dough into big circles. She cooks one in the oven. Lola adds tomato puree to passata to make the filling. She also adds some canned tuna, green olives, and boiled egg. She seasons the mixture with paprika, black pepper and her special ingredient, oregano. Then Lola spreads the filling over the dough, which has been in the oven. She finishes the recipe by adding the last piece of dough on top and then putting it back in the oven to cook. Will Lola's friends find the empanadas tasty? Will they guess her special ingredient? There's a very exciting Spanish surprise waiting for her friends while they enjoy their meal!

programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. There has been a special sleep over event at Safari World, where visitors had the chance to see all the nocturnal animals. Andy heads to the Aqua Base to find out how it went. Jen informs Andy that it has been a huge success and that Mr Hammond is just taking the visitors to see the last of the animals, the cephalopods. Mr Hammond calls and is still wearing his stripy pyjamas. He is having trouble spotting the common octopus, which disappeared the moment she saw him. Jen uses a special sonar device, which can detect objects under the water, but it is not able to detect the missing octopus. Southampton are looking to complete a double over the Seagulls, having come back from behind to prevail 2-1 when the sides met at Amex Stadium in December thanks to goals by Jannik Vestergaard and Danny Ings, but Brighton have generally been slightly stronger on their travels this term. With analysis from Dion Dublin

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Ireland recorded a 19-12 victory when the sides met at Aviva Stadium in last season's competition, with Jonathan Sexton scoring the only try of the game kicking his side's other 14 points, and they also prevailed 22-13 here in 2019, but the Scots have produced some strong recent performances and will be hopeful of revenge. Gabby Logan presents, with analysis from John Barclay, Martin Johnson and Andy Nicol, and commentary from Eddie Butler, Chris Paterson and Jamie Heaslip

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison joins Adam Henson and his team for 48 hours of hectic lambing. She may be the rookie in Adam's lambing shed, but Ellie quickly gets to grips with multiple births, touch-and-go adoptions, and the emotions of the life-or-death decisions faced every day during this busiest of spring seasons on the farm. Tom Heap is on the case to find out why mountains of wool, historically the nation's most valuable sheep product, are now piling up in warehouses across the country

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in London. Treasures include a pair of hands crafted by Lucian Freud, a destination board from the front of an early London bus, and a model of Donald Campbell's Bluebird, which broke the land-speed record in 1964. There is also a rare set of wedding jewellery from Nepal, and a diamond cross and gold necklace with a hefty price tag

programme.title: Match of the Day 2

Spurs prevailed 2-0 over their North London rivals in December's reverse fixture thanks to goals by the deadly duo of Heung-Min Son and Harry Kane, but the Gunners will have been determined for revenge on home soil. United had already defeated the Hammers at home in the FA Cup and also prevailed 3-1 in the league at London Stadium. Plus, Leicester City v Sheffield United and Southampton v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a dog groomer who wants to relocate her business to Suffolk on a budget of £320,000. He also meets the team behind an ambitious project to restore an Anglo-Saxon longship, the remains of which were unearthed by an amateur archaeologist in the 1930s

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Hertfordshire friends Adam and Dan both follow a vegan diet, so today's ingredients for Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang include everything from vegan staples to more exotic produce.
