programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy restorers Amanda and Julie revive a sad dog that has been badly burned in a devastating house fire, metal expert Dominic tries to get a rusty retro kitchen mixer beating again and silversmith Brenton works his magic on a nurse's belt buckle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon asks Kathy how Gavin died and is shocked when she realises Kathy is planning to go to the memorial service - as far as she is concerned, he may have been her biological father but he can rot in hell. Peter finds himself caught in the middle when Ash and Suki argue, and Bailey finds a stray dog - but Karen says they cannot afford to keep it

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki tells Ash she should do anything necessary to get her job back, so Peter assures her he will say he gave himself the medication. Mitch explains that Bailey is heartbroken over losing Bronson so Rainie enlists Stuart's help to find the stray dog. As an emotional Sharon packs up her things up in the Vic, Kim tries to organise her a leaving party

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira tells Hanssen that he may not have been Reyhan's only victim, leading the great surgeon to become distracted while operating on her father. Chloe realises Ange is missing and grows increasingly suspicious that Cameron has something to do with her disappearance. Louis gets into an altercation with a former friend who has turned up seeking revenge

programme.title: Roman Kemp: Our Silent Emergency
programme.desc: Last August, Roman Kemp's life changed dramatically with the sudden and unexpected death of his best friend, radio producer Joe Lyons. It led him to question why increasing numbers of young men are taking their own lives and why so many of them never ask for help. Here, Roman sets out to explore what can be done to encourage people to seek help, what preventative action others need to be taking, and the lasting impact of mental health and suicide on friends and family

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Sheriff Romero responds to Norma's 911 call and has a surprising proposal for her concerning the deaths of Keith Summers and Zack Shelby. Even with those recent events now behind them, Norma reacts badly to Dylan's decision to move into his own place. The hotel is not quite open yet but gets its first guest Jake Abernathy, who claims to have been a regular when Summers owned the place

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is upset after Emma tells some of the other girls that he and Bradley slept together. He leaves school without permission, and in a meeting with the principal and his teacher it is suggested to Norma that Norman should seek professional help. The session with the psychologist does not go well as the patient shows signs of severe strain

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain hurdling great Colin Jackson, former England striker Darren Bent, ex-WBA Super Middleweight champion boxer George Groves and Commonwealth Games gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Fulham v Manchester City at Craven Cottage and Leeds United v Chelsea at Elland Road. The Citizens will have been expecting to maintain their excellent form at the expense of struggling Fulham, while the Blues will have hoped to emulate their 3-1 win over Leeds at Stamford Bridge in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v West Bromwich Albion and Everton v Burnley

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Jess Phillips MP, shadow minister for Domestic Violence and Professor Sir Ian Diamond, national statistician and head of the ONS. With News Review by Ayesha Hazarika, Evening Standard, Alex Forsyth, BBC political correspondent and Kelly MacDonald, Line of Duty actor

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: This week presenters Rev Dr Colin Dow of Glasgow City Free Church and Rev Dr Sonia Blakesley of St Mark's Oldhall, Paisley continue Lent thoughts considering one of the most famous verses in the Bible; John 3:16. Music is by Rachel Hair and Adam Rhodes

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Southampton v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Premier League encounter at St Mary's Stadium. Southampton are looking to complete a double over the Seagulls, having come back from behind to prevail 2-1 when the sides met at Amex Stadium in December thanks to goals by Jannik Vestergaard and Danny Ings, but Brighton have generally been slightly stronger on their travels this term. With analysis from Dion Dublin

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Ireland recorded a 19-12 victory when the sides met at Aviva Stadium in last season's competition, with Jonathan Sexton scoring the only try of the game kicking his side's other 14 points, and they also prevailed 22-13 here in 2019, but the Scots have produced some strong recent performances and will be hopeful of revenge. Gabby Logan presents, with analysis from John Barclay, Martin Johnson and Andy Nicol, and commentary from Eddie Butler, Chris Paterson and Jamie Heaslip

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison joins Adam Henson and his team for 48 hours of hectic lambing. She may be the rookie in Adam's lambing shed, but Ellie quickly gets to grips with multiple births, touch-and-go adoptions, and the emotions of the life-or-death decisions faced every day during this busiest of spring seasons on the farm. Tom Heap is on the case to find out why mountains of wool, historically the nation's most valuable sheep product, are now piling up in warehouses across the country

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in London. Treasures include a pair of hands crafted by Lucian Freud, a destination board from the front of an early London bus, and a model of Donald Campbell's Bluebird, which broke the land-speed record in 1964. There is also a rare set of wedding jewellery from Nepal, and a diamond cross and gold necklace with a hefty price tag

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Tom Brannick enlists the help of Heather Pentland to find out what Jackie Twomey knows about Goliath. Tori Matthews and Tom's daughter Izzy put pressure on Tom to get to the truth. With lives on the line, Tom must unmask Goliath before it's too late. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v West Ham United at Old Trafford. Spurs prevailed 2-0 over their North London rivals in December's reverse fixture thanks to goals by the deadly duo of Heung-Min Son and Harry Kane, but the Gunners will have been determined for revenge on home soil. United had already defeated the Hammers at home in the FA Cup and also prevailed 3-1 in the league at London Stadium. Plus, Leicester City v Sheffield United and Southampton v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Married couple Sam and Louise from Aberdeen take on The Wall, hoping to combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Will they get the questions right? And can they walk away with cash to buy a bigger house for when they can start a family? Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.desc: Luca's past creates trouble for him when Jonno tells all to Zara. Bear is alarmed to realise a care service for a teenage boy is just a front for something more sinister

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah meets a couple from Warwickshire who have a dream of owning a property in north-west Wales that would provide them with a home and the ability to offer holiday lets

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Jeremy Pang and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by friends Paul and Roberto, who have high-pressure jobs and like to barbecue together to relax

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Will Kirk takes on the huge responsibility of restoring a model ship, carefully handcrafted out of matchsticks by a beloved father who died when his children were young

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As the government faces mounting criticism that well-connected people made millions out of Britain's PPE crisis, Panorama investigates who won out. More than £12billion was spent in the first six months of the pandemic on contracts to provide personal protective equipment. Reporter Richard Bilton meets one man who made £40million on a deal, and speaks to others who felt ignored in favour of less experienced suppliers. As the government refuses to reveal the full details of all its so-called VIP deals, the programme reveals the high-profile connections to one lucrative contract

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea is nervous as the smuggling job looms, and to make things worse Caleb arrives with an extra package to carry, once again threatening her to make sure all goes to plan. Kathy receives an unexpected phone call, Ash's likes catch up with her and Bailey is heartbroken as the Taylors say a tough goodbye. Over at the Vic, Sharon has found a new place to live as Mick and Linda's flat has come back onto the market

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: In the first episode of the classic sitcom, beleaguered and badgered hotelier Basil Fawlty tries to attract a better class of clientele, but ends up being taken to the cleaners by the first seemingly upper-crust customer to check in. Starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales, Andrew Sachs and Connie Booth

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the latest heat, as five more hopefuls get their first chance to win an apron by cooking their Signature Dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. After tasting all five plates, John and Gregg pick their favourite dishes, and those cooks will earn themselves not only a MasterChef apron but also a place in the next round

programme.title: Pretty Single
programme.desc: Michaela considers dating completely make-up free in a bid to find her perfect match

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' acting skills are put to the test, with EastEnders' Natalie Cassidy giving them a masterclass in the art. Michelle Visage and Graham Norton are on the judging panel, alongside an extra special guest judge

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain hurdling great Colin Jackson, former England striker Darren Bent, ex-WBA Super Middleweight champion boxer George Groves and Commonwealth Games gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Martin Roberts look around properties in Blackpool, Derby and the West Midlands town of Bilston, before hearing how they have been redeveloped

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers keep an eye out for coach drivers who refuse to take a break despite being exhausted, and track down two fraudsters running a sim swap scam

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help two teams buy items at Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits the Museum of Timekeeping in Newark

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel tries his best to end the hunger strike once and for all. A young woman in Ruhma's care is warned not to eat during labour but her overbearing father has other ideas

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a dog groomer who wants to relocate her business to Suffolk on a budget of £320,000. He also meets the team behind an ambitious project to restore an Anglo-Saxon longship, the remains of which were unearthed by an amateur archaeologist in the 1930s

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Hertfordshire friends Adam and Dan both follow a vegan diet, so today's ingredients for Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang include everything from vegan staples to more exotic produce. They both hope to walk away with some great new recipes - but who will leave as the winner? Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the culinary contest

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy restorers Amanda and Julie revive a sad dog that has been badly burned in a devastating house fire, metal expert Dominic tries to get a rusty retro kitchen mixer beating again and silversmith Brenton works his magic on a nurse's belt buckle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon asks Kathy how Gavin died and is shocked when she realises Kathy is planning to go to the memorial service - as far as she is concerned, he may have been her biological father but he can rot in hell. Peter finds himself caught in the middle when Ash and Suki argue, and Bailey finds a stray dog - but Karen says they cannot afford to keep it

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki tells Ash she should do anything necessary to get her job back, so Peter assures her he will say he gave himself the medication. Mitch explains that Bailey is heartbroken over losing Bronson so Rainie enlists Stuart's help to find the stray dog. As an emotional Sharon packs up her things up in the Vic, Kim tries to organise her a leaving party

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira tells Hanssen that he may not have been Reyhan's only victim, leading the great surgeon to become distracted while operating on her father. Chloe realises Ange is missing and grows increasingly suspicious that Cameron has something to do with her disappearance. Louis gets into an altercation with a former friend who has turned up seeking revenge

programme.title: Roman Kemp: Our Silent Emergency
programme.desc: Last August, Roman Kemp's life changed dramatically with the sudden and unexpected death of his best friend, radio producer Joe Lyons. It led him to question why increasing numbers of young men are taking their own lives and why so many of them never ask for help. Here, Roman sets out to explore what can be done to encourage people to seek help, what preventative action others need to be taking, and the lasting impact of mental health and suicide on friends and family

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary investigates one of Northern Ireland's largest criminal gangs - one which trades in drugs and murder but stands under the banner of loyalism - South East Antrim UDA

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Sheriff Romero responds to Norma's 911 call and has a surprising proposal for her concerning the deaths of Keith Summers and Zack Shelby. Even with those recent events now behind them, Norma reacts badly to Dylan's decision to move into his own place. The hotel is not quite open yet but gets its first guest Jake Abernathy, who claims to have been a regular when Summers owned the place

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is upset after Emma tells some of the other girls that he and Bradley slept together. He leaves school without permission, and in a meeting with the principal and his teacher it is suggested to Norma that Norman should seek professional help. The session with the psychologist does not go well as the patient shows signs of severe strain

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by comic actress Amy Poehler, star and director of the rebellious new comedy Moxie and comedian Rob Beckett, who is promoting his podcast Lockdown Parenting Hell. Call the Midwife's Leonie Elliott also guests, alongside Hollywood star Jennifer Garner and singer-songwriter Nick Jonas. With music from singer-songwriter Tom Odell

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of House of Commons proceedings

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: BBC Wales journalist Elliw Gwawr visits Highgate Cottage in Llanystumdwy, the childhood home of First World War premier David Lloyd George

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee present the best of the action from the second day of the Scottish National Sheep Dog Trial in Brisbane Glen Estate, West Kilbride. The weather holds, but it's a difficult course for the handlers. A variety of sheep means it's the luck of the draw what packet the handlers get, with some being more stubborn than others. Good runs from Jimmy Menzies and Ross Henderson put them in a good position to make the team

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is peeling off old wallpaper on Linda's walls, but he is actually destroying Fizz' hypnotic posters.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo finds himself shrunk and then dragged inside the President's head, along with Fizz. He thinks the hamster is a little, lost boy and decides to help him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is on a camping trip but he is actually just in the city park. He mistakes Weasel for a wounded bear and tries to help him.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: An endless cacophony of loud noises from next door drives Mr Bean to distraction, filling his mind with thoughts of revenge

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean pretends he's been the victim of an accident in the hope of gaining attention, but ends up in hospital

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike aims to impress Iris by showing off his acrobatic moves on a trampoline, but the raccoons see it as the perfect escape route for their mischievous plans

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Jessica Cruz tries to find a home for Dexter, an unadoptable cat with a secret power.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Blackfire begins her plan to conquer Earth the girls must pull out all the stops to defeat he

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave finds a handheld computer game at the dump which involves killing zombies - his friend Zombie Katie, however, finds the game rather insulting. Exacting her revenge, Dave finds himself confronted with a group of very angry and very real zombies.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on her favourite countryside walk through the rolling plains of Cheshire, a trail with eight counties on show, two castles to explore and spectacular views across to Wales and Liverpool. A popular route for dog-walkers and sports, her trek proves to be full of surprises

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes actor Dawn Steele and opera singer Lesley Garrett to the farm. With lambing season in full swing there are some new arrivals to be seen, and there is also a look forward to St Patrick's Day with some world-class Irish cocktails

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond shares his love of travel and embracing the influence of other cultures into his cuisine, including a fresh flash-fired squid with fennel and rocket salad, and a miso and harissa aubergine with cous cous. Plus, chef and good friend José Pizarro visits and indulges Raymond with a dish of pan-fried salted cod with chickpeas, white beans and spinach stew

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: With the support of fiance Michael Brown, Preston mum Roisin Pelan is facing stage 3C cancer. Despite enduring challenging treatment, she has raised thousands for charities and is determined to see young daughter Ivy grow up. Alan Titchmarsh, David Domoney, Frances Tophill and Katie Rushworth proceed to turn their dangerous sloping garden in Preston, Lancashire, into a stunning music festival-inspired haven

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Catastrophe star Rob Delaney, cricket veteran Graeme Swann and comedian Holly Walsh take on each other as well as the arcade-style machine in the hope of winning £20,000 for charity. Ben Shephard hosts

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Jacqui Oatley, Dev Griffin, Fallon Sherrock and Johnny Vegas pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The Greatest Show on Ice draws to an epic conclusion as the final three celebrities battle to be crowned this year's champion of the rink. They'll each have to perform a showcase choreographed by Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean and recreate their favourite routine of the series, but just two couples will get to perform their own take on the Bolero. There's an extra special performance by Torvill and Dean, and the full class of 2021 return to the ice for one last time

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Roy Grace's career is at rock bottom and is in the last chance saloon running enquiries into long-forgotten cold cases with little prospect of success. Then he is approached by colleague DS Glen Branson to help investigate the disappearance of a Brighton property developer, who vanished without a trace on his stag night. Crime drama, starring John Simm and Richie Campbell

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: The Week That Shook The Royals
programme.desc: In the week after Meghan and Harry's headline-grabbing interview with Oprah Winfrey airs, ITV's Royal Editor Chris Ship talks to commentators about the fallout from the sit down, explores the impact on the royal family and asks what change the couple are hoping will emerge from their heartfelt revelations

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry goes on a camping trip to Brownsea Island with his family, where he plans to catch and cook dinner for the whole family. After taking his grandson fishing, he heads for a cookery masterclass with head chef of the nearby Rick Stein restaurant. The next day, Harry and his son, Jamie Redknapp compete in a game of crazy golf

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents action from the fourth round of fixtures, featuring Scotland v Ireland at BT Murrayfield, England v France at Twickenham and Italy v Wales at Stadio Olimpico. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi, Gordon D'Arcy and Jim Hamilton

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Brands Hatch, featuring round 15 of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB and round 16 of the Ginetta Junior Championship

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five family members from Merseyside answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will covers for Harriet's absence. Faith is suspicious of Mack. Kim gloats about her meddling

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Harvey threatens to put Leanne and Simon in hospital unless he gets all of the money he is owed. Tyrone flirts with Alina in the cafe and it's clear she enjoys his attention. Sally insists on helping Abi plan her wedding and appoints herself Maid of Honour

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a garden for a family with twin girls who have a serious, life-limiting condition. Three-year-olds Darcie and Evie Chapman have been diagnosed with Dravet syndrome, a very rare form of epilepsy, and parents Mark and Natalie must be on permanent watch to prevent their daughters' seizures leading to something more serious. The Chapmans' current outdoor space is unsuitable for the girls, with changes of levels, colours of plants and even the light through branches influencing their condition, so the crew follows the rules of a formal garden where straight lines, order and symmetry give an unfussy overall look

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne receives a text from Harvey with the details of her first drugs drop. Asha confesses to Dev that it was her idea to buy the pills, and he is shocked at her level of despair. Michael's grateful when Ed insists he'll pay for the party

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny discover Walsh was chased by all four suspects on the night in question. Willets realizes Fiona's blood sample may have been deliberately lost by Liz. The pathologist identifies a cause of death implying Walsh was murdered

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alastair Stewart presents extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including how a pod of hungry killer whales had its hopes dashed when a plucky young seal seized a life-saving opportunity when a tourist boat of whale watchers sailed close by. Plus, the gripping journey a group of sightseeing friends from Boise, Idaho made when a miscalculation in the air density caused its small aircraft to crash into woodlands shortly after take-off. And the inspirational story of an adventurous Isle of Wight resident who took to the skies in a sponsored tandem skydive for charity - at the grand old age of 91

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall gives more members of the public a chance to tackle a deeply personal challenge, recording their progress as they seek to complete it within the space of 12 months. This time, she meets a grandfather determined that this will be the year he finally learns to read and write, a girl with big ambitions for starting school, and a man searching for the stranger who saved his life not once, but twice

programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present the opening day of the prestigious festival, including feature race the 3.05 Unibet Champion Hurdle Challenge Trophy, plus races at 1.20, 1.55, 2.30 and 3.40. With analysis from AP McCoy, Mick Fitzgerald and Ruby Walsh, reports by Alice Plunkett and Luke Harvey, betting news from Matt Chapman, social stable chat with Oli Bell and Chris Hughes, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith sneaks into the farm to find evidence. Cain reveals the truth to Moira. Will lies Harriet is away on a retreat

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights Norfolk has to offer. Ben Fogle takes to the Broads on a traditional Norfolk wherry boat, while in the flat wetlands of the Fens, Liz Bonnin checks out some rare eight-legged wildlife. Elsewhere, Olympian Victoria Pendleton joins the Queens Mounted Regiment on their annual holiday at Holkham beach

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes in which two sports stars test their survival instincts, former rugby union player Jonny Wilkinson joins Bear Grylls to explore Dartmoor's harsh and gruelling terrain, an adventure that will push his physical and mental limits. From climbing 40 metres up a sheer cliff face to crossing ice cold rivers, Jonny also learns the key basic survival skills necessary to survive in remote terrain like Dartmoor

programme.title: 2020: The Story of Us
programme.desc: Oscar-winning director Kevin Macdonald's feature-length documentary tells the story of coronavirus in Britain through the experience of people immersed in its impact, both personally and professionally. Using testimony from staff in hospitals - consultants, doctors and nurses - as well as weaving in the experience of patients, this documentary paints a vivid portrait of the working lives of ICU staff and those they treat during a crisis which proves both challenging, life-changing and often tragic for those caught in it

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has her work cut out as she tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How a homemade guitar amp changed the course of music history overnight and how a team of expert pen makers are giving a Second World War fighter a new lease of life

programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave finds a handheld computer game at the dump which involves killing zombies - his friend Zombie Katie, however, finds the game rather insulting. Exacting her revenge, Dave finds himself confronted with a group of very angry and very real zombies.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on her favourite countryside walk through the rolling plains of Cheshire, a trail with eight counties on show, two castles to explore and spectacular views across to Wales and Liverpool. A popular route for dog-walkers and sports, her trek proves to be full of surprises

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes actor Dawn Steele and opera singer Lesley Garrett to the farm. With lambing season in full swing there are some new arrivals to be seen, and there is also a look forward to St Patrick's Day with some world-class Irish cocktails

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond shares his love of travel and embracing the influence of other cultures into his cuisine, including a fresh flash-fired squid with fennel and rocket salad, and a miso and harissa aubergine with cous cous. Plus, chef and good friend José Pizarro visits and indulges Raymond with a dish of pan-fried salted cod with chickpeas, white beans and spinach stew

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: With the support of fiance Michael Brown, Preston mum Roisin Pelan is facing stage 3C cancer. Despite enduring challenging treatment, she has raised thousands for charities and is determined to see young daughter Ivy grow up. Alan Titchmarsh, David Domoney, Frances Tophill and Katie Rushworth proceed to turn their dangerous sloping garden in Preston, Lancashire, into a stunning music festival-inspired haven

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Catastrophe star Rob Delaney, cricket veteran Graeme Swann and comedian Holly Walsh take on each other as well as the arcade-style machine in the hope of winning £20,000 for charity. Ben Shephard hosts

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Jacqui Oatley, Dev Griffin, Fallon Sherrock and Johnny Vegas pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The Greatest Show on Ice draws to an epic conclusion as the final three celebrities battle to be crowned this year's champion of the rink. They'll each have to perform a showcase choreographed by Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean and recreate their favourite routine of the series, but just two couples will get to perform their own take on the Bolero. There's an extra special performance by Torvill and Dean, and the full class of 2021 return to the ice for one last time

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Roy Grace's career is at rock bottom and is in the last chance saloon running enquiries into long-forgotten cold cases with little prospect of success. Then he is approached by colleague DS Glen Branson to help investigate the disappearance of a Brighton property developer, who vanished without a trace on his stag night. Crime drama, starring John Simm and Richie Campbell

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: The Week That Shook The Royals
programme.desc: In the week after Meghan and Harry's headline-grabbing interview with Oprah Winfrey airs, ITV's Royal Editor Chris Ship talks to commentators about the fallout from the sit down, explores the impact on the royal family and asks what change the couple are hoping will emerge from their heartfelt revelations

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry goes on a camping trip to Brownsea Island with his family, where he plans to catch and cook dinner for the whole family. After taking his grandson fishing, he heads for a cookery masterclass with head chef of the nearby Rick Stein restaurant. The next day, Harry and his son, Jamie Redknapp compete in a game of crazy golf

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents action from the fourth round of fixtures, featuring Scotland v Ireland at BT Murrayfield, England v France at Twickenham and Italy v Wales at Stadio Olimpico. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi, Gordon D'Arcy and Jim Hamilton

programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Brands Hatch, featuring round 15 of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB and round 16 of the Ginetta Junior Championship

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Harvey threatens to put Leanne and Simon in hospital unless he gets all of the money he is owed. Tyrone flirts with Alina in the cafe and it's clear she enjoys his attention. Sally insists on helping Abi plan her wedding and appoints herself Maid of Honour

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a garden for a family with twin girls who have a serious, life-limiting condition. Three-year-olds Darcie and Evie Chapman have been diagnosed with Dravet syndrome, a very rare form of epilepsy, and parents Mark and Natalie must be on permanent watch to prevent their daughters' seizures leading to something more serious. The Chapmans' current outdoor space is unsuitable for the girls, with changes of levels, colours of plants and even the light through branches influencing their condition, so the crew follows the rules of a formal garden where straight lines, order and symmetry give an unfussy overall look

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne receives a text from Harvey with the details of her first drugs drop. Asha confesses to Dev that it was her idea to buy the pills, and he is shocked at her level of despair. Michael's grateful when Ed insists he'll pay for the party

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny discover Walsh was chased by all four suspects on the night in question. Willets realizes Fiona's blood sample may have been deliberately lost by Liz. The pathologist identifies a cause of death implying Walsh was murdered

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alastair Stewart presents extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including how a pod of hungry killer whales had its hopes dashed when a plucky young seal seized a life-saving opportunity when a tourist boat of whale watchers sailed close by. Plus, the gripping journey a group of sightseeing friends from Boise, Idaho made when a miscalculation in the air density caused its small aircraft to crash into woodlands shortly after take-off. And the inspirational story of an adventurous Isle of Wight resident who took to the skies in a sponsored tandem skydive for charity - at the grand old age of 91

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall gives more members of the public a chance to tackle a deeply personal challenge, recording their progress as they seek to complete it within the space of 12 months. This time, she meets a grandfather determined that this will be the year he finally learns to read and write, a girl with big ambitions for starting school, and a man searching for the stranger who saved his life not once, but twice

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights Norfolk has to offer. Ben Fogle takes to the Broads on a traditional Norfolk wherry boat, while in the flat wetlands of the Fens, Liz Bonnin checks out some rare eight-legged wildlife. Elsewhere, Olympian Victoria Pendleton joins the Queens Mounted Regiment on their annual holiday at Holkham beach

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes in which two sports stars test their survival instincts, former rugby union player Jonny Wilkinson joins Bear Grylls to explore Dartmoor's harsh and gruelling terrain, an adventure that will push his physical and mental limits. From climbing 40 metres up a sheer cliff face to crossing ice cold rivers, Jonny also learns the key basic survival skills necessary to survive in remote terrain like Dartmoor

programme.title: 2020: The Story of Us
programme.desc: Oscar-winning director Kevin Macdonald's feature-length documentary tells the story of coronavirus in Britain through the experience of people immersed in its impact, both personally and professionally. Using testimony from staff in hospitals - consultants, doctors and nurses - as well as weaving in the experience of patients, this documentary paints a vivid portrait of the working lives of ICU staff and those they treat during a crisis which proves both challenging, life-changing and often tragic for those caught in it

programme.title: ITV News at Ten

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has her work cut out as she tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Happy Feet (2006)
programme.desc: A young penguin disappoints his fellow seabirds with his inability to sing - but instead he displays a remarkable talent for tap dancing. Animated adventure, with the voices of Elijah Wood, Robin Williams, Nicole Kidman and Hugh Jackman

programme.desc: Meg takes advantage of Peter's temporary job as principal of her school to get even with her bullies, while Brian and Stewie open a bed and breakfast

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna decides to run for a place on the school board and keeps her husband away from the campaign in case he embarrasses her. Junior discovers he has a natural talent as a diver, but his chances of success are threatened when he is too shy to wear the school's tight swimming trunks

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: The Browns pack their bags and move to Los Angeles, where Cleveland plans to make his dream of being a baseball scout a reality. However, his new job fails to live up to expectations, and when he befriends struggling actress Gina (guest star Krysten Ritter), he discovers that life in California is not quite as glamorous as it seems. Meanwhile, Donna launches a new career as a children's entertainer

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The chefs compete in the inaugural Spell's Kitchen challenge, with a private pool party the reward. Dinner service caters for important charities, but who will be going home?

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A teenager rides her hoverboard into a swimming pool, a man gets his beard caught in a power tool and a drone crashes into an unfortunate child's face

programme.desc: When Amy has to bring her baby to the store, she grows frustrated with people's criticisms of her parenting and Glenn enlists Jerusha to help. Garrett helps Dina plan her first date with her boyfriend, while Sandra and Cheyenne hatch a conspiracy theory

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The family sets out on a trip to New York in a bid to win back Chris's place in a Scout group, but Peter takes a wrong turn en route and ends up at a Native American casino - where Lois becomes obsessed with the slot machines. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris volunteers to take care of his Mexican girlfriend's twin babies, but after learning that she cannot come back to the US, the Griffins go to Mexico to reunite her with her children

programme.title: American Dad!

programme.desc: Omnibus. Steve reacts angrily when Peter lets him down. Ken offers Simon a sympathetic ear. Kirk falls victim to Sean's selling scheme

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Gabby is horrified as realisation sinks in, Ellis questions Priya's motives, and Nicola resolves to take back control

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell return to help more people reunite with long-lost relatives, beginning with a man whose last contact with his father was a brief phone call from America when he was six, and a woman who was transported from Bogota to Berkshire when her Colombian birth mother gave her up for adoption

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress flies over Tokyo in a helicopter. The city was bombed extensively during the Second World War, so almost all of it is a symbol of the post-war economic boom that saw Japan become the world's second largest economy. While in the capital, Joanna heads out to a nightclub to see a Japanese girl band and witnesses the largely male audience perform almost as much as the artists on stage. Later, Joanna travels to the Kiso Valley to walk the Nakasendo Way, an ancient route that once linked Tokyo to Kyoto, a place best known for that most famous of Japanese traditions, the Geisha

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When psychologist Reuben Beatty is murdered while moonlighting as a psychic, Lewis and Hathaway struggle to unravel the victim's baffling double life. Dark forces appear to be at work, and not just in the afterlife. Sanjeev Bhaskar guest stars, with Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Clare Holman and Rebecca Front

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers Nick has been brutally assaulted - and she is top of the list of suspects. But even worse, she has no recollection of the previous night after a drunken hen party, and struggles to convince herself she did not do it. Janet thinks she has made her peace with Andy, until he gives her some misinformation that results in Gill missing an appointment with the coroner - so the DCI, tired of their games, announces one of them will have to go. A man's accidental death turns into a murder investigation when his daughters reveal their mother often talked of killing him. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Called to the home of an elderly couple who haven't been seen for a while, Janet discovers Eunice Bevan's severed head at the bottom of the stairs, her body at the top, and her husband Joe in bed, emaciated and barely able to speak. Meanwhile, the detective puts her house on the market, admitting her relationship with Ade is over, and three months in to Rachel's marriage to traffic cop Sean, she's starting to feel a little claustrophobic. George Costigan and Nicola Walker guest star, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Gabby is horrified as realisation sinks in, Ellis questions Priya's motives, and Nicola resolves to take back control

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe becomes hysterical at the thought of the Malletts taking Katie away. Kevin resolves to win Sally back. Ashley feels caught in the middle between Zoe and the Malletts

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy learns the truth about the robbery. Viv talks to Alan about going to identify Vic. The Dingles wonder what to call the baby

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy pleads with his dad to give himself up. Mandy and Paddy make friends again. Kelly tells Scott as far as she is concerned their kiss never happened

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate is confronted by a young pregnant village girl while practising as a partner with the elderly local GP. Nick takes a call saying that a flasher has been sighted on the moors. Nick Berry stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass attempts to turn events to his advantage when a remote farm is raided, and a visitor from London evokes bad memories. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Vic's funeral arrives. Chris receives a summons to appear as Kim's defence witness. Ned threatens Eric when he gossips about the Sugdens

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak declares he wants to be a house husband. Viv cannot cope running the post office by herself. Mandy is jealous that Paddy and Tricia are getting on well

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz turns to Jim for help when she thinks everything is falling apart for her. Liam tells the Malletts they can have Katie back for 2,000 pounds

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: With Zoe gone, Ashley asks Maxine to move back in. Natalie attempts to make Kevin jealous. Zoe does not trust Liam and runs away from the squat, taking Shannon with her

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Major Charles Ingram doesn't like quizzes, but for his wife Diana and brother-in-law Adrian knowing the answer is a way of life. As Diana and Adrian's repeated attempts to get into the hotseat on Who Wants to Be a Millionaire? fall short, all eyes turn to an unsuspecting Charles to win the million-pound prize. Meanwhile, the repeated reappearance of middle-class, professional quizzers has not gone unnoticed by the ITV team and producer Paul Smith makes a vow to stop them by any means necessary. Fact-based drama directed by Stephen Frears, starring Matthew Macfadyen, Sian Clifford and Mark Bonnar, with Michael Sheen as Chris Tarrant

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: In a retrospective episode set immediately after the events of series two, Rachel faces further accusations of masterminding the fatal attack on ex-lover Nick and finds comfort in the arms of ever-faithful Sean, agreeing to marry him. Amid the wedding celebrations, Janet and Ade admit defeat and decide to split up for good, while Gill is duty bound to take action when she catches Pete in a compromising position. The detectives also investigate the case of a businessman suspected of murdering a homeless man

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Liz turns to Jim for help when she thinks everything is falling apart for her. Liam tells the Malletts they can have Katie back for 2,000 pounds

programme.desc: With Zoe gone, Ashley asks Maxine to move back in. Natalie attempts to make Kevin jealous. Zoe does not trust Liam and runs away from the squat, taking Shannon with her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Vic's funeral arrives. Chris receives a summons to appear as Kim's defence witness. Ned threatens Eric when he gossips about the Sugdens

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak declares he wants to be a house husband. Viv cannot cope running the post office by herself. Mandy is jealous that Paddy and Tricia are getting on well

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass attempts to turn events to his advantage when a remote farm is raided, and a visitor from London evokes bad memories. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates come up with a new rule to give their romantic pursuits a chance of privacy - but it means giving 24 hours' notice. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick interrupts an armed robbery at the Aidensfield Arms and ends up being held hostage in the cellar with Greengrass. Meanwhile, the local newsagent is found to be selling Swedish pornography. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lyn starts work at the vets. Rachel gets an invitation to visit Steve at the remand centre. Kelly warns Terry to stay away from Viv. Laura invites Chris for a romantic meal

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah worry about going back to the village. Zak and Butch take Belle to the betting shop. Kathy tells Kim she is unsure if Steve hit her deliberately or not

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is stunned when Jon tells her he cannot spend all of Christmas Day with her. The Battersbys attempt to talk Les out of killing their Christmas turkey

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary tells Judy he is thinking of leaving her. Les is furious to find out Toyah has freed the turkey. Liam tracks Zoe down and tells her his duplicitous plan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Barnaby is dragged along to the wedding of the son of Sarah's friend Hazel, the last thing he expects to find himself doing is investigating a murder. But when his wife discovers the body of the bride, Laurel, who has been squeezed to death by her own wedding dress corset, the detective must look more closely at his fellow guests. The victim's husband Gavin is convinced that his wife's death had everything to do with her outspoken nature on her radio show, so Winter's attention soon turns to her unique community of nocturnal listeners. Gavin's scorned ex-fiancee and local wedding dress designer Serena also comes under suspicion, and Barnaby and Winter's enquiries lead them to a local wedding fayre. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Kelly Brook, Nick Hancock and Liz Fraser

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Charles returns for a second night in the hot seat with a new strategy. After his previously hopeless performance, he proves massively successful, his dithering answers invariably turning out to be right. However, the production team find his sudden change of fortunes suspicious, and begin looking into an unusual pattern of coughing from the studio audience

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother makes plans for her daughter's future happiness - and she knows just the man to help. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Steve reacts angrily when Peter lets him down. Ken offers Simon a sympathetic ear. Kirk falls victim to Sean's selling scheme

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Gabby is horrified as realisation sinks in, Ellis questions Priya's motives, and Nicola resolves to take back control

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster

programme.desc: Charles returns for a second night in the hot seat with a new strategy. After his previously hopeless performance, he proves massively successful, his dithering answers invariably turning out to be right. However, the production team find his sudden change of fortunes suspicious, and begin looking into an unusual pattern of coughing from the studio audience

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Big Fish Off

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey present the competitive angling show, joined by ex-Liverpool and Spurs footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock and former middleweight boxing champion Steve 'the Celtic Warrior' Collins. They all head back to Ali's fishing roots in Essex as the journey turns into one of humour, drama, and plenty of coarse fish, as well as a crazy sea fishing challenge to finish

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The skippers reveal their secrets as they take viewers inside their world with engine fails, new technology, expensive machinery and epic sea disasters

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: Luke and his team are contracted to remove two stolen vehicles from an abandoned mine site that has been turned into a recreational lake for weekend campers and water skiers. The missing cars are toxic and they have a 15,000-dollar budget and two days to get the job done

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An elderly widow enlists Holmes' help to ascertain why a mystery purchaser should want to buy not just her house, but also all its contents. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke and Peter Wyngarde

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A man dies suddenly of a mysterious fever and Holmes suspects murder, but a malign influence takes its toll on the detective as he delves into the underworld of London's docklands. Starring Jeremy Brett, with Roy Hudd

programme.title: Red River (1948)
programme.desc: Conflict grows between a tough Texan rancher and his Civil War veteran foster son during a 1,000 mile cattle drive to Missouri. The older man's uncompromising methods and unyielding outlook lead his workers to mutiny - and the son ends up siding with the rebellious ranch hands against his father. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Montgomery Clift, Walter Brennan, Joanne Dru and Coleen Gray

programme.title: Red River (1948)

Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Montgomery Clift, Walter Brennan, Joanne Dru and Coleen Gray

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: First of a two-part drama, which sees CS Forester's seafaring hero crossing swords with tyrannical Captain James Sawyer, an officer on the brink of madness. Reprimanded for falling asleep while on watch, young Horatio partly redeems himself by saving the Renown from seemingly certain disaster _ but ends up charged with mutiny. Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay and David Warner star

programme.title: Cycling: Paris-Nice Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the eighth and final stage, featuring a hilly 110.5km course around Nice. Gary Imlach presents, with commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents action from the fourth round of fixtures, featuring Scotland v Ireland at BT Murrayfield, England v France at Twickenham and Italy v Wales at Stadio Olimpico. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi, Gordon D'Arcy and Jim Hamilton

programme.title: A Good Day to Die Hard (2013)
programme.desc: John McClane goes to Russia having received word that his estranged son Jack is in trouble with the law. He is surprised to discover Jack is secretly a CIA agent, and father and son join forces to take on the Russian Mob, who are engaged in a plot to steal nuclear weapons. Action adventure sequel, starring Bruce Willis and Jai Courtney

Thriller sequel, with Charles Bronson, Lesley-Anne Down, Robert Joy and Michael Parks
