programme.desc: The docudrama focuses on the machinations of ambitious aristocrat the Earl of Essex as he tries to take over Robert Cecil's spy network in an attempt to gain control over the ageing Elizabeth I and hold the power to choose the next king of England

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Elizabeth is dead and James VI of Scotland travels south to take the throne at the invitation of Robert Cecil, but the new king has his own ideas and has little time for the late queen's spymaster. Soon Cecil's problems worsen when learns that his arch enemy John Gerard has escaped detention and made contact with a splinter cell in the Catholic underground with a plan to blow-up Parliament. Cecil must negotiate between warring factions to bring down what would become known as the Gunpowder Plot

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Nicholas Day explores the case of Edith Thompson, who conspired with her lover to murder her husband in 1922 - a story that inspired Alfred Hitchcock

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: Divers search a newly discovered wreck as they investigate the story of the SS Justicia, sunk in the First World War by U-Boats off the coast of Ireland

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: A marine biologist and a deep-sea diver explore Chuuk Lagoon, one of the world's greatest underwater graveyards, for a wreck discovered by sonar nearly 20 years ago

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on his final journey in London, exploring Albertopolis in south Kensington and reaching dizzying heights inside a Victorian landmark. At Vauxhall, he learns about the darker side of London's flower market in Bradshaw's day and ends at London Bridge, where two stations are becoming one and a new concourse is being built

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new journey following his Bradshaw's handbook from the heart of the industrial East Midlands to the island of Lindisfarne off the coast of Northumberland. On the first leg, he gives an old engine a fresh start in the railway hub of Derby, heads to Nottingham to find out about the Victorian origins of a well-known high-street chemist and calls at a historic one-time grocer's shop in Grantham, Lincolnshire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Lyme Park in Cheshire, famously the location for the 1995 TV adaptation of Pride and Prejudice. Items brought in to be valued include a pair of pistols used to protect royal mail coaches from highwaymen, a tea caddy disguised as a pile of books, and a portrait of actress Sarah Bernhardt that once belonged to Elton John

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn goes behind the scenes at London's King's Cross station and finds out about the disused funicular railway hidden in the Avon Gorge in Bristol, blasted out of the cliff

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A two-hour special featuring a deserted Caribbean city, an American ghost town still burning and an Arctic settlement facing a terrifying future

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The story of a bear that fought alongside the Polish army, which became a terrifying modern myth among the German army during the Second World War

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill's plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Lyme Park in Cheshire, famously the location for the 1995 TV adaptation of Pride and Prejudice. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, with this leg beginning in Boston, south Lincolnshire, where he explores the connection between the town and its American namesake. At Southwell, Nottinghamshire, he discovers the origins of a favourite Victorian apple before heading into West Yorkshire to visit an imposing institution built in Menston to provide asylum for those with mental health issues. In nearby Wakefield, Michael boards one of Britain's least frequent services, before heading to Hensall, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey in the East Yorkshire town of Hessle, where he learns about the technology that made the Humber Bridge possible. In nearby Hull, Michael hears from local MP Alan Johnson about anti-slavery campaigner William Wilberforce, before travelling to Scarborough Castle, where the founder of the Quaker movement was once imprisoned. His last stop of the day is York, where he learns what made the city a centre for the sweet-making industry

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Graphic designer Alpha takes his turn to model for the group. Abstract artist Jilly sees something in Alpha's choice of pose that nobody else does. Jon tries out a different drawing style, while Steph experiments with an unusual tool to add texture to her work. Presented by Jenny Eclair

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Gaming enthusiast Chris is the last model of the week, as the artists compete for the final chance to enter the winners' gallery. Just one place remains before the group decide which is the best life drawing produced by the class, and Jenny crowns the Drawers Off artist of the week. Hosted by Jenny Eclair

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Gaming enthusiast Chris is the last model of the week, as the artists compete for the final chance to enter the winners' gallery. Just one place remains before the group decide which is the best life drawing produced by the class, and Jenny crowns the Drawers Off artist of the week. Hosted by Jenny Eclair

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Catching up with a couple who opened a cycle retreat in Spain and two gite owners who got more than they bargained for after starting a holiday business in France three years ago

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Debbie from Staffordshire would love to find a holiday home on the beautiful island of Fuerteventura where they can enjoy spending quality time together. They're looking for a two-bedroom apartment near the attractive coastal town of Caleta de Fuste and have a budget of £115,000. Ben Hillman shows them five properties from them to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bedfordshire couple Dean and Karen Dhinsa find a rural retreat in southern Tenerife. The pair dream of spending their retirement in a sunny climate, and are looking for a home within easy reach of the island's beaches, complete with a cave to transform into a cinema, all on a budget of £180,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Celia Thompson has long dreamed of running an arts and crafts business in the Carcassonne area of France. However, her partner Nick Hares is hesitant about leaving the UK, as his teenage daughters live near Cambridge. He'd rather move to the Waveney Valley, on the border of Suffolk and Norfolk. Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton help the couple solve their dilemma

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dry-cleaning manager Valerie hosts the first dinner party in Lancashire, where she plans to impress her guests with a lamb hotpot and trifle. However, it remains to be seen whether her warm-hearted hospitality will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize by the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dance teacher Nicky hosts the second night in Lancashire, where she hopes to impress her guests with a round-the-world themed menu inspired by her dancing tours, which includes a Chinese salmon starter, and an American dessert. However, with fellow contestant Peggy born in the United States, the host may have her work cut out in presenting the perfect dish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Outsourcing manager Steve hosts the third night in Lancashire, where the 51-year-old plans to wow his guests with a 'man of the world' menu that relies heavily on strong Indian spices. Peggy picks him up on his shop-bought black pudding, while Nicky is unimpressed by the mildness of the chicken balti, resulting in a humiliating chilli-sauce challenge

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: American Peggy hosts a USA-themed dinner party on the fourth night of the competition in Lancashire, and hopes her Arizonan recipes will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize. Her menu comprises tried and tested dishes, including her dessert of peach dump cake. However, friction between her and fellow contestant Steve continues to build

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party in Lancashire, and the turn of sales manager Andy to host. He is confident in his locally sourced gastro menu and delivers a meal with plenty of flair, but faces the challenge of impressing foodie Nicky. He is also under pressure to convince American Peggy of the merits of the region's food, while his fellow Lancastrians Valerie and Steve are keen to see how the area has influenced his cooking

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary and Janet are ready to take early retirement together in eastern Spain, so Laura Hamilton shows them five properties in Orihuela for their budget of £140,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman shows five properties to recently retired West Sussex couple Alison and Martin, who are looking to buy a holiday home along Spain's Costa Dorada

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney hosts the grand final of the pottery contest, which turns the clock back to the 1920s with a series of art deco-themed challenges. The contenders must make a punch bowl and decanters and face the tiniest throwing challenge the show has ever set. Judges Rich Miller and Keith Brymer Jones are on board to assess the efforts and crown this year's winner

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Wood Green animal charity, who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. An eight-stone Newfoundland makes a favourable first impression on the Coxon family, but staff are worried that his habit of showering everyone with a spray of slobber may take some of the gloss off. Nine-week-old French bulldog Kevin competes with a nervous terrier to win the hearts of loved-up couple Ryan and Katie, while a recently widowed grandmother seeks another 'beating heart' to help fill a home that is feeling very empty

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A young man who has had a severe reaction to a recreational drug is wheeled into resus by paramedics, and senior sister Jen and the medical team face a challenge to calm their patient down so they can treat him. A 65-year-old woman is brought in by her best friends after hitting her head when she fell off a chair while playing bingo, and a 31-year-old man with multiple sclerosis is rushed in after developing a fever at home

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Oprah with Meghan and Harry and the subsequent news that Piers Morgan had resigned as co-host of Good Morning Britain. Other programmes being considered include Paranormal Lockdown UK, The Celebrity Circle for Stand Up to Cancer, Teen First Dates, Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby and BBC News coverage of NHS pay

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A 23-year-old virgin from Kent picks a partner from six nude candidates - but this proves a nerve-racking experience, since he has never seen a naked woman in his life. Meanwhile, a 27-year-old beauty therapist from London is determined to have a baby before she turns 30, and hopes that one of the men lurking behind the screens will be the perfect father

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: The charity version of the popular baking show returns, and with Noel Fielding on paternity leave, Matt Lucas is presenting on his own. In previous editions, comedian Tom Allen has stepped in to co-present - but today he is showing off his baking skills as one of the contestants, alongside Star Wars actress Daisy Ridley, comedian Rob Beckett and singer Alexandra Burke. To impress Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, the celebrities serve up millionaire shortbread, a fruity tart and their pet hates in cake form in the showstopper

programme.title: Let's Talk About Sex
programme.desc: The first of three programmes drawing on contemporary and archive footage to explore the nation's changing attitude to sex and sex education through the ages. It features parents and their children - including celebrities with their offspring - as they watch, review and react to outrageous, astounding and unintentionally hilarious sex education videos covering everything from puberty to pregnancy. The first episode focuses on how sex education handles basic biology, from managing menstruation to the rules of reproduction and the challenges of masturbation. Contributors include Danny Dyer, Miriam Margolyes, Alastair Campbell, London Hughes and Ulrika Jonsson

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two bungalows requiring different solutions. In Purley, Croydon, Christine Attwood and Tony Congdon have been going back and forth for almost a decade about the future of their unusual bungalow. In Larne in Northern Ireland, Catherine and Nigel Finch have hit a spot of turbulence due to their dated, 1970s-style home. Nigel enjoys the rural location's incredible views, but Catherine has never loved the place and misses having neighbours, so Phil searches for a home that pleases them both

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge's to-do list is as long as ever, and with no guests, weddings or events at the chateau this year, they use their engineering and design skills to tackle some major projects. First on the list is transforming one of the old outbuildings into a supersized workshop for chateau-sized jobs

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Jenny Eclair hosts a competition in which amateur artists compete to win a cash prize by creating life portraits of one another

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Cameras revisit two pensioners struggling to bring their favourite British pastime to France and reveal how two twenty-somethings got on launching an Irish bar on the Costa del Sol

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After holidaying in Spain for the past decade, London couple Desiree and Pal feel now is the time to buy their own holiday home around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca and have a budget of £65,000 at their disposal. Jasmine Harman helps the pair find their ideal property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bedfordshire couple Liz and Grahame Pink, who have saved hard for their dream holiday home in Lanzarote and have a budget of £63,000 to spend on a property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Karin Lorenz would love to start a new life in south-western France. She wants to find the right house, with scope to run a self-catering business, so she can get it underway before moving there. However, her partner Mike Griffin would prefer to live in Pembrokeshire. Whichever destination they plump for, Karin's mother is set to move with them - as will their two dogs. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the pair some tempting properties

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Training guru Steve Carlyle hosts the first party in York, but a sophisticated evening, complete with a butler, does nothing to boost his reputation as he competes to win the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trevor Rooney, who leads tours of supposedly haunted sites in York, hosts the second dinner party in the city at a bric-a-brac warehouse. He also has several spooky surprises in store for his guests, including a magic show, a paranormal-themed menu and a ventriloquist's doll that spends the evening flirting with the female diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third dinner party in York is hosted by Annie Albericci, who hopes to bring peace, love and harmony to the table with her festival-chic-themed evening. However, things go from bad to worse for Annie when the male guests find a racy photo of her

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Yvonne McGill hosts the fourth dinner challenge in York and goes for a 1960s-themed party, including a game of Snog, Marry, Avoid? and a Beatles tribute band. Meanwhile, guest Steve reveals his celebrity crush

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Foodie Rebecca Ryan hosts the final dinner party in York, and hopes to impress her guests by throwing a Hollywood-themed evening of glitz and glamour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Eagle Rock in California to try to change the fortunes of Capri, a grubby restaurant which often remains closed during lunchtime because of the twin owners' lazy attitude. A crisis ensues as soon as Gordon Ramsay instructs the duo to clean the place, revealing that only tough measures are likely to save the business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bettina and Eamon have spent nearly 30 years in military accommodation moving with Eamon's job. They are now ready to start the next chapter of their lives by buying the first property they will have bought together. They have chosen the Costa Calida in Spain and are hoping one of the five properties Danni Menzies has found within their £100,000 budget prove be perfect for them

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Photographer Nat and budding artist and writer Ruth from Leicester fell in love with Tarn in France on holiday and were so inspired by the region's quaint villages and beautiful scenery that they now want to buy their very own holiday home there. Laura Hamilton lines up five properties for their budget of £90,000, but will any of them be the perfect place?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The host helps Leicester couple James and Drew restore their period house, reclaiming its classic Georgian dimensions and features. In Brixton, south London, a Victorian living room has had all its period features ripped out and replaced with 1970s kitsch and fake Tudor. Can George turn this travesty into a smart and sophisticated family sitting room? Plus, he visits a 300-year-old silk weaver's house in Spitalfields, London, that is perfectly adapted for stylish modern living

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge's to-do list is as long as ever, and with no guests, weddings or events at the chateau this year, they use their engineering and design skills to tackle some major projects. First on the list is transforming one of the old outbuildings into a supersized workshop for chateau-sized jobs

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Oprah with Meghan and Harry and the subsequent news that Piers Morgan had resigned as co-host of Good Morning Britain. Other programmes being considered include Paranormal Lockdown UK, The Celebrity Circle for Stand Up to Cancer, Teen First Dates, Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby and BBC News coverage of NHS pay

programme.title: Two Sisters, One Body
programme.desc: The story of Mexican conjoined twins Carmen and Lupita Andrade, who are omphalopagus - meaning they are attached at the lower abdomen, exploring the challenges they face on a daily basis. At the girls' birth, their parents Norma and Victor faced an impossible dilemma - attempt separation, where only one twin would survive, or stay together. With experts in the United States, the family travelled to Connecticut, where 17 years later they remain on a discretionary visa programme, near the medical facilities that are crucial for their continued quality of life

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Matt Lucas presents as four more famous faces put their baking skills to the test for charity. Hollywood star James McAvoy, singer and Voice coach Anne-Marie, writer and comedian David Baddiel and former athlete Kelly Holmes are this week's quartet, whose challenges involve decorative tarts, a savoury technical and rendering animals in cake form

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets entrepreneur Paul Wilkinson and his wife Amy, who are building a giant new home on a 16-acre plot in Lincolnshire. The superstructure will be comprised of five circular buildings made up of interlocking timber cylinders, linked by raised wooden walkways that hang over a fishing lake. The complicated build proves difficult for Paul's local team, and their ambitious schedule of completing the project in a year soon looks out of reach

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Amateur footage and eyewitness accounts telling the story of the earthquake and subsequent tsunami that caused devastation in Japan in March 2011. The film also discovers how the country dealt with the aftermath of these natural disasters, as people struggled to rebuild their lives and communities

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Wood Green animal charity, who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. An eight-stone Newfoundland makes a favourable first impression on the Coxon family, but staff are worried that his habit of showering everyone with a spray of slobber may take some of the gloss off. Nine-week-old French bulldog Kevin competes with a nervous terrier to win the hearts of loved-up couple Ryan and Katie, while a recently widowed grandmother seeks another 'beating heart' to help fill a home that is feeling very empty

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: Grayson and Philippa Perry are joined by TV presenter Sue Perkins, who crafts an unusual work of art on the subject of food. Grayson also talks to Rose Wylie, who became an art world star in her seventies. There's a peek inside the studio of Lucy Sparrow, who uses felt to create colourful replicas of everyday food items, while comedian Mawaan Rizwan makes his own piece of food-related art. Plus, special guest Nigella Lawson helps choose the favourite public art submissions of the week

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Observational series following rich kids who move in with families struggling to make ends meet

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Cases include Wendy, who was left with rental arrears of almost £3,000 after renting a house out to a nightmare tenant. Steve from Birmingham Council's housing enforcement team investigates poor living conditions above a local shop. And in south London, lawyer Chris has to deal with his client's site which has been filled by nearly two dozen caravans and a mountain of rubbish

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Watford builder David Simpson has a fight on his hands when his long-term tenant moves her boyfriend in and suddenly decides to stop paying - and when he tries to evict her, she makes life difficult. In Woolwich, lawyer Chris Sharpe helps a client whose property has been taken over by squatters, who have changed the locks and claim they are legitimately renting the house

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Teorin and Collette had high hopes when they let out their five-bedroom family home, but it wasn't long before their tenant stopped paying rent - and then turned the house into a hostel for holiday-makers. Quickly running out of options, the couple turn to eviction specialist Chris Sharpe for help. Plus, Harrow council officers discover 40 people crammed into a potential deathtrap of a house, and a woman who relies on her rent for medical expenses tries to claw back some from her tenant

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Susan and Gary bought an East End flat to let out and were approached by a letting agent who promised hassle-free guaranteed rent. But before they knew it, he had broken in and changed the locks. Now they are fighting to get the property back. Meanwhile, landlord Paul discovers that his seriously ill father's long-standing tenant owes a staggering £20,000 in rent for living in her Notting Hill home

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The tale of an elderly couple whose hopes of a peaceful retirement were ruined when their tenants stopped paying rent, then moved in their abusive friends who became squatters. With the house becoming a free-for-all and in a terrible state of repair, and the couple facing a barrage of verbal threats and abusive behaviour, they have been forced to seek help in an attempt to get their property back. Plus, a man who cannot sell his house because his sitting tenant refuses to leave

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Enforcement officers are faced with the difficult job of evicting a woman with two children - a task that takes a turn for the worse when her ex-partner makes an appearance. In Liverpool, locals threaten a riot over an unpaid debt at a computer shop, while two agents come across an extremely rare car in the course of their duties

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul, Steve and Ben make a shocking discovery when they break into a home in Essex while serving an eviction notice on a single mother and her two children. Meanwhile, Stewart and Iain receive more than just threats from the staff and owner of a taxi business, a car dealer has to choose between paying up or losing his showroom stars, and an Indian restaurant owner is in danger of losing his livelihood

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve make an extraordinary discovery inside a locked bedroom, and immediately call for police back-up. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town football club to recover a debt, while Paul and Ben have difficulty evicting a couple from a house in Kent after learning they have six children and nowhere else to go

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian O'Shaughnessy and Graham Aldred's first case sees them in a Brighton health spa as they search for assets to seize in pursuit of a £20,000 debt. Following a stand-off, they devise a way to work with the owner and her professional advisers to come up with a solution. Later, Graham reveals his softer side when he revisits a woman who defaulted on a debt payment plan

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage to provide accounts of extraordinary disputes, including the story of Sarah, whose life was destroyed by her neighbour's son

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A relationship between two couples in Brighton that took a dramatic turn when one couple's son joined the other family's firm. In Paisley, Sylvia's evening with her neighbour ended in a fight for her life

programme.title: The Killing of James Bulger
programme.desc: On 12th February 1993, a CCTV camera captured footage of two young boys leading a two-year-old child away from the Strand shopping centre in Liverpool. This two-part documentary tells the definitive story of the case, examining in forensic detail what happened on that tragic day, how the young killers were caught and the devastating legacy of the murder on James's family and the community. With access to his mother Denise Fergus, his brothers speaking exclusively for the first time, along with the police and others closely involved in the case

programme.title: When Kids Kill
programme.desc: The heartbreaking story of young couple Sophie and Robert, who were the victims of a vicious attack because they dressed like Goths

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: American serial killer Bill Suff, who is thought to have murdered between 12 and 22 people during an 18-year spree. Described as a mild-mannered loner, Suff raped, stabbed, strangled, and sometimes mutilated prostitutes in California, beginning in 1986. In January 1992, he was arrested after a routine traffic stop

programme.title: The Disappearance
programme.desc: Susan and Charles focus their investigation on one suspect, and a new clue proves a turning point in getting closer to the truth

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Documentary following emergency response teams on their most extreme medical callouts

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Meeting bargain and bartering supremo Tony Carey and his wife Claire, who moved onto the Saydo Park community less than a year ago and bought their mobile home for an absolute steal. There is also a visit to familiar faces Joe and Jan, who have been married for 47 years, and handed over their animal charity last year to their friend Jacqui, in a bid to try and enjoy their retirement. Then the unthinkable happened, when 69-year-Jan was diagnosed with breast cancer

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces across the country to reveal how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions against shoplifters. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Sophie, a privileged woman who loves cash rather than kids, is sent to stay with Pete, Mandy and five youngsters. She also has to help Pete in his job, working with children

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams and inspectors out to nail the litter louts and the fearless sewage warriors clearing the mess

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In Bristol, eviction lawyer Chris deals with a complex case in which squatters have broken into an empty pub and are now refusing to leave, with landlord Alex wanting them out as he is awaiting planning permission to turn the pub into flats. Jill is trying to regain a property she and her late husband bought as an investment, which she has been renting out to alleviate financial stress

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Harrow Council carries out early morning raids on several dangerous properties, only to find vulnerable tenants in uninhabitable conditions, including one man living in a plywood shed on a roof. Plus, two more cases of nightmare tenants. Nisha lost her job just as her tenants stopped paying the rent, while Andy agreed to rent his north London home to a friend - who ended up subletting the house and pocketing the profits for himself

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Twenty officers descend on a hotel to arrest members of a criminal gang in their beds, Jon and Maya raid a cannabis farm, and Craggy and Phil catch a serial bacon snatcher. Three police cars and a helicopter take on a teenage van thief, Sgt Carlin stops a driver who has a gun hidden behind his seat, and Brian reduces a time-wasting motorist to a blubbering wreck

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Members of the operational support unit of Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit smash through a bedroom window in a drugs raid, a speeding driver is clocked at over 140mph, and friendships are pushed to the limit when amphetamines are found in a car. Plus, vehicle thieves try - and fail - to outpace the interceptors, and an officer gets help from a very unexpected source

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Mark gets lucky when a car thief crosses paths with him, there is a high-speed pursuit through Lincoln city centre, and a member of the public helps the interceptors track down a drink-driver. Elsewhere, Jon Peacock gets an unusual request and Jon Moon has an encounter with a surprising lawbreaker

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection. Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy try to collect a debt from a technology company, but with the director nowhere to be seen, the office manager does everything she can to prove the business is no longer trading. Stewart McCracken and Elmor Victor face a heartbreaking house repossession in Merseyside, where two landlords are forced to evict a tenant recovering from a brain tumour which has cost him his job and income

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy get into a doorstep confrontation with a debtor who wants to prove her family business is bankrupt. However, when tempers flare Brian is assaulted and the agents call the police. Elsewhere, Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner face evicting people from three properties owned by the same landlord in north London, and Brian and Delroy Anglin collect £2,000 debt owed to a vet in Kent

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Nicolette's tough love approach seems to finally work for Chloe. Brent struggles at school, prompting Terese to try lifting his circumstances

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Tori gets called in to resolve Christian's dispute, and Jasmine shares that Lewis believes his negligence caused the death of his wife

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Among the UK's wealthiest 10 per cent, the Scaife-Lacy family from Beverley in East Yorkshire trade places with the Flinthams, who live just six miles away in Hull and are in the country's poorest 10 per cent. They swap homes, budgets and lives for seven days as they see what it is like at the other end of the economic spectrum

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall helps identical twins who have been inseparable all their lives, but are now struggling because one sister's labrador cross is always bullying the other's rescue dog. Other clients include a French bulldog who hates TV and is always smashing holes in the set, and a sprocker who is constantly destroying things around the house

programme.title: Cats and Dogs at War
programme.desc: Nanci and Chirag face their biggest challenge yet with two households where there is real fear that recently arrived big dogs could injure the resident cats

programme.title: Extreme Fare Dodgers
programme.desc: With special access to Transport for London's revenue protection teams, this documentary tells the stories of frontline enforcement officers and undercover investigators fighting back against passengers dodging fares across the English capital ever day

programme.title: Adults Only
programme.desc: Cameras take a look at the business of life-sized adult dolls, companies such as Silicone Lovers, and the . Bromsgrove woman who sells and rents plastic sex companions

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Critical care paramedic Rob and doctor Adam fight to save the life of a boy who has been stabbed in the street. The 16-year-old has been knifed in the neck, has lost a significant amount of blood and is struggling to breathe. Elsewhere, Tom Waters is dispatched to 30-year-old bricklayer Daniel, who has sliced his arm with a power saw. He was cutting timber on a workbench using an angle grinder with a circular blade when he lost control and it sliced through his right hand

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A patient brought into Hull Royal Infirmary by police claims to have swallowed drugs, but he refuses to co-operate with staff, putting them under further pressure. In resus, a patient's life hangs in the balance as the team assess the extent of a serious brain bleed, which could be life-altering, while a 17-year-old arrives after injuring his ankle at a skate park

programme.title: Me & My Sugar Daddy
programme.desc: Women in their 20s who make money from having relationships with men over 50, including 25-year-old Mimi, who has earned thousands of pounds from sugar daddies

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Devon, 19, leaves her parents' eight-bedroom, 12-bathroom mansion in Exeter to live with the Gibbins family of Lincoln, who struggle to make ends meet

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams and inspectors out to nail the litter louts and the fearless sewage warriors clearing the mess

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby. Plus, an east London landlord who found half a dozen strangers living in his house, after his original tenant had run off and sublet the property. Meanwhile, Harrow Council raid a small two-bedroom flat in unsafe conditions

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with the victims and villains of the housing rental market. Rachel intended to rent out her Snowdonia cottage for only three months. Four years later her tenant is still there and not paying any rent. Plus, the stories of Stevenage landlord Derek, whose tenant Kellie proved so troublesome that the police had to be called, and Claud from south London, who was left £30,000 in debt when a professional couple split up

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve discover a locked wooden case when they are called to evict tenants from a flat in London's Docklands. Opening it up, they realise they urgently need police back-up - and the property quickly becomes a crime scene. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town FC to collect a debt, while Paul's team arrives at a property in Essex to find that they have to remove a woman and her six children

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team carries out a raid that turns up a stash of drugs, a weapon and a large pile of cash, while Jon Peacock is amazed to encounter a half-naked man covered in blood on the wintry streets of Lincoln, but the real surprise comes when the officer finds out what he has been up to. Suspicious driving leads Jim Woolerton to discover super-strength lager and drugs, and a speeding motorist comes out with a very middle-class excuse after being clocked travelling at 110mph

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw tracks down a driver who has given him the slip on the side roads of Spalding, while Suzie Ranyard comes to the rescue of a motorist with a rather large passenger. Jim Woolerton utilises some of the unit's most advanced kit to catch out a disqualified driver and officers are called out when a dead body is spotted in one of Lincolnshire's many waterways

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embarks on a desperate manhunt amid fears that an armed and dangerous character is roaming the streets of Lincoln, while Craggy closes in on a suspected shoplifter with a bag full of surprises. Officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit also tackle a would-be stunt rider and a boozed-up biker who is raising hell on the A-roads

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner get caught up in a dispute between a landlord and a tenant who refuses to move out, and Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin chase a £17,000 debt. In Essex, a couple owe their neighbour £11,000, and on the south coast, debt collection from a Turkish restaurant gets complicated when there is confusion about ownership

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In east London, High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Phil are involved in a brothel eviction, while in south Wales, Stewart and Elmore chase a debt owed by a working mum. Another case sees Stewart and Victor hunting down a £3,000 debt owed by a Liverpool takeaway for rent arrears

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Susan realises Olivia Bell is back in town, and it seems Ms Bell wants to make amends. Kyle has money problems and needs to find a way to afford the rent

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John invites Susie for a steamy time, not knowing she is digging for information and snooping through his finances. Ari tries convincing Chloe to give her mum another chance

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, new restrictions have provoked mixed reactions, while an angry parker and his brother turn the air blue in Edinburgh as they try to find a space for Burns Night. A bodybuilder enrages his girlfriend and angry parents by parking in a parent-and-child space, arguing he is too big to squeeze out of his car in a standard bay

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A one-off edition of the documentary in which journalist Nick Wallis reveals how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions, focusing on criminals in London involved in robberies, thefts, drug crimes, street brawls and weapons offences. In Soho, police officers monitor the activity of the area's 100,000 nightly visitors from a CCTV control room in an underground bunker, while in Lambeth the air support unit is called in when an armed gang are seen rampaging through the streets

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A patient brought into Hull Royal Infirmary by police claims to have swallowed drugs, but he refuses to co-operate with staff, putting them under further pressure. In resus, a patient's life hangs in the balance as the team assess the extent of a serious brain bleed, which could be life-altering, while a 17-year-old arrives after injuring his ankle at a skate park

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Critical care paramedic Rob and doctor Adam fight to save the life of a boy who has been stabbed in the street. The 16-year-old has been knifed in the neck, has lost a significant amount of blood and is struggling to breathe. Elsewhere, Tom Waters is dispatched to 30-year-old bricklayer Daniel, who has sliced his arm with a power saw. He was cutting timber on a workbench using an angle grinder with a circular blade when he lost control and it sliced through his right hand

programme.title: The Rainmaker (1997)
programme.desc: An idealistic newly qualified lawyer teams up with a disreputable researcher to champion the cause of a leukaemia sufferer whose mother is suing an insurance company for refusing to pay his medical costs. As the case progresses, the young legal eagle finds he has much to learn about the dark side of the judicial process. Francis Ford Coppola's courtroom drama adapted from the novel by John Grisham, starring Matt Damon, Danny DeVito, Jon Voight, Claire Danes and Mickey Rourke

programme.title: Columbo: Death Hits the Jackpot (1991)
programme.desc: A photographer going through a divorce asks his jeweller uncle to cash in his winning lottery ticket to prevent his wife from getting a share. Seizing the opportunity to keep the money, the greedy older man kills his nephew - but has reckoned without Columbo's intervention. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Rip Torn, Gary Kroeger and Jamie Rose

programme.title: Columbo: Murder by the Book (1971)
programme.desc: An author of mystery novels kills his former writing partner so he can claim sole credit for their collaborations, and tries to appear innocent by offering to help Columbo solve the crime. Detective drama, directed by Steven Spielberg, starring Peter Falk, Jack Cassidy, Rosemary Forsyth and Barbara Colby

programme.title: Columbo: Candidate for Crime (1973)
programme.desc: An ambitious senatorial candidate murders his campaign manager for threatening to expose his secret affair. To avoid suspicion, the politician makes the killing look as if he was the intended target of an assassination attempt that went wrong - but ends up having to pit his wits against the slovenly sleuth. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Jackie Cooper, Ken Swofford and Tisha Sterling

programme.title: Columbo: Sex and the Married Detective (1989)
programme.desc: A sex therapist tries to pin the blame for the murder of her unfaithful lover on her assistant, but the dishevelled detective is not convinced by the ruse and seeks to identify a mysterious woman in black who was spotted leaving the scene of the crime. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Lindsay Crouse, Peter Jurasik and Julia Montgomery

programme.title: Columbo: Strange Bedfellows (1995)
programme.desc: A horse trainer outraged by his brother's gambling debts murders both him and the bookie he owes money to, then claims one killed the other. Columbo is not convinced by his story and goes in search of the truth, and finds help from an unlikely source when a Mob enforcer is sent to investigate the bookie's death. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Rod Steiger and George Wendt

programme.title: Columbo Undercover (1994)
programme.desc: The detective dons various disguises to investigate the deaths of two men who apparently killed each other, his only clue being a fragment of a black-and-white photograph, and comes to suspect the double murder has a connection to a $4million bank robbery. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Ed Begley Jr, Burt Young and Tyne Daly

programme.title: Columbo and the Murder of a Rock Star (1991)
programme.desc: The crafty detective pits his wits against a brilliant defence lawyer who has never lost a case. The attorney has murdered his rock star girlfriend to prevent her exposing his unethical courtroom tactics, and then framed her lover for the crime. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Little Richard, Dabney Coleman, Shera Danese, Cheryl Paris and Julian Stone

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A spiritual healer is accused of sexually assaulting a client while they are hypnotised

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is attacked in her own home, and the man who once stalked her becomes the prime suspect. Guest starring Chris Bauer (True Blood)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a port authority officer reveals Parsa's possible whereabouts as the team continues the hunt for the terrorist intent on wiping out NCIS. Bishop reveals her past association with the quarry and suggests where she thinks he may be found, but the CIA has intercepted a call that places Parsa in a different location to Ellie's hunch. Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A blogger who accused the NCIS of a cover-up is found dead, and Gibbs and his team are forced to reopen a closed case to find the killer. However, state police suspect the special agents may have been involved in the murder and take control of the investigation. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a marine off the coast of Tanzania - a case complicated by the discovery that he had a link to Ziva. The mystery deepens even further with the recovery of wreckage from a sunken freighter. Starring Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when lawyers Fred and Lizzy Bellamy are found murdered in their sleep. The case leads them to two couples who are in the process of adopting children from Haiti, but the detectives uncover signs of abuse that point to a human-trafficking ring

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a plaintiff involved in a high-profile lawsuit against airline Pan World is found dead in his car. The detectives soon discover the case had been delayed after the presiding judge was murdered and the clues lead to a lawyer willing to do anything for money

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Logan suspect a missing infant may have been killed by her parents rather than kidnapped, and their inquiries lead them to uncover a terrible family secret relating to a mentally ill serial killer. Award-winning police drama, starring Chris Noth and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when an illegal immigrant is attacked, and the incident is connected to a spate of hate crimes against Hispanic men. The prosecution of some teenagers for the crime proves to be a thorny and delicate issue for the District Attorney's office

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murders of a firefighter and his wife. The case leads to one of the dead man's colleagues, whom the detectives suspect resorted to extreme measures to cover up the crime - but prosecutors at the district attorney's office discover that taking the suspect to court could have serious political repercussions

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A gay city councillor is shot dead after a meeting, and the trail of suspicion leads the team to a bigoted rival politician and a male prostitute. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the Halloween murder of a marine with a fondness for playing practical jokes. As the evidence accumulates, they begin to suspect he was killed by the exasperated victim of one of his pranks. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of two dishonourably discharged officers are found on board a boat belonging to Gibbs, and inquiries cast suspicion on his mentor, retired agent Mike Franks. The case proves to have wider implications - and the team finds itself battling to prevent an international confrontation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team delves into Vance's troubled past in a desperate effort to trap a vengeful female assassin named Lee Wuan Kai. Although she is determined to strike back at him by targeting his family, it gradually transpires that there is another deeper motive for her crimes. Crime drama, guest starring Kelly Hu, with Mark Harmon, Rocky Carroll and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee and Bishop travel to Afghanistan after a murder case in the States is linked to a string of soldiers killed by friendly fire overseas. Kasie leaves the lab and accompanies Torres into the field to process additional forensic evidence. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray and Wilmer Valderrama

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers faulty bulletproof vests in an abandoned truck filled with stolen USMC merchandise, and attempts to locate the source of the cargo and any vests that could have been sent to military personnel overseas. Crime drama, guest starring Challen Cates, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo confronts a man impersonating a Navy officer, which results in guns being drawn, and the only eyewitness to the case is the Special Agent's father - who is in town to disclose a secret to his son. The 250th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when Harold and Joyce Foley arrive home to find their son and housekeeper murdered. The detectives start to believe the couple's work on a controversial pharmaceutical study made them targets, until further evidence changes their minds

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a trucking company owner is found murdered. They suspect his business partner Stan Klein is the culprit when they discover his links to the Russian mob and a scheme to defraud the victim, but as the case comes to court it seems Klein's son may have had reason to kill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan and Briscoe deal with a double homicide when an abused alcoholic breaks into his parents' former home and murders the current occupants. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murders of two scientists found stabbed to death following a fire. The evidence points to a mentally disturbed woman, but Cutter and Rubirosa suspect she may have been manipulated by someone else

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The chief suspect in the murder of several homeless men appears to be a schizophrenic street performer. Detectives Lupo and Bernard's problems compound when they find evidence suggesting a possible copycat is at work. Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A gay city councillor is shot dead after a meeting, and the trail of suspicion leads the team to a bigoted rival politician and a male prostitute. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance insists on taking charge of an investigation into the fatal shooting of a boxer - but antagonises Gibbs in the process, and finds his own past coming under scrutiny as the case unfolds. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is discovered during a city-wide power cut - and because Jethro and the team are unable to use their modern electronic equipment, they have to solve the case the old-fashioned way, and it soon becomes apparent that national security could be at stake. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The marine son of a colonel-turned-preacher is found dead in woodlands after changing his religious persuasion. As the case unfolds, it becomes apparent to the agents that he could have been the victim of a hate crime. Meanwhile, Gibbs' father visits for the holidays. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren reflects on his youthful sporting endeavours as he tries to track down the killer of a high-school basketball star who had gone missing and the private investigator hired to find him. Interviews with his classmates and friends reveal shady links to gambling syndicates. Drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.desc: Barba takes on the case of a man accused of raping a woman in his church group, but his investigation is restricted by laws guaranteeing freedom of religion

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Part one of two. A Muslim family is targeted in a hate crime, but Barba is forced to release a suspect when a witness is deported

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

After three months the team returns to review their progress

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: Fantasy Homes by the Sea
programme.desc: Jenni Falconer helps two friends with a budget of £250,000 who are hoping to buy a property in Puglia, Italy

programme.title: Fantasy Homes by the Sea
programme.desc: Jenni Falconer helps a couple who dream of idyllic family holidays in Cornwall and have a budget of £500,000 to find a property in the region

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Linnette and Sandra Genus from west London are part of a large extended family spread across the UK, USA and Caribbean. They're now looking for somewhere to host family get-togethers and have chosen the west coast of Florida as the perfect spot. Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties for their £160,000 budget in and around the city of Fort Lauderdale

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Geri Melville is originally from St Lucia, but moved to the UK when she was just nine. Now, she's ready to return to her roots with her husband Ian, by buying a holiday home together on the island she left almost 50 years ago. With a budget of £360,000 Laura Hamilton shows them five properties boasting views of the Caribbean Sea

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman assists Pat and Anya Rich, who are looking for a holiday home on Florida's southern Gulf Coast where they can spend family time with their seven-year-old daughter Sofia. With a budget of £150,000, Ben shows them five impressive properties, but will any of them live up to their dream?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Anne-Marie McNeill from Scotland have fallen in love with the buzzy holiday atmosphere of south Tenerife after visiting regularly for seven years. Now they're looking for a second home where they can enjoy family holidays with their two children, so Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £200,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Reg and Sarah Etwaroo from East Sussex are buying their first ever place together, and have their hearts set on a second home in the theme park capital of the world - Orlando in Florida. Ben Hillman shows the recently married couple properties for their £100,000 budget

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton set off on a trip around the south of Scotland as they seek out bargains that will earn them a profit. They begin their journey in Glasgow, which Philip soon flees in favour of the Western Isles, before the pair are reunited again at auction in Edinburgh

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell aims to catch up on the second leg of the journey, as he and James Braxton cross the border from Scotland and head toward an auction in Kendal on their antique-finding mission

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and James Braxton travel around the north-west of England on the third leg of their road trip, each hoping to make a resounding profit on the items they take to auction. Philip visits the birthplace of Liverpool's most famous band, and James relives his childhood as he looks into the story of scouting

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and James Braxton start the fourth day of their journey in Kingsley, Cheshire, before travelling to an auction in Liverpool. One of the antiques experts has a commanding lead, leaving the other desperate to find a way back into the competition before the final leg tomorrow

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton begin the fifth and final leg of their road trip in Wales in search of bargains, and it's all to play for as they head for an auction showdown in Newport, Shropshire

programme.title: Secret Eaters
programme.desc: Anna Richardson puts another overweight family under 24-hour surveillance to scrutinise their eating habits. The Castles from Welwyn Garden City in Hertfordshire cannot understand why they are putting on weight when they eat mum Belinda's healthy, home-cooked food. But the proof of the pudding is in the hours of footage that reveals what they really snack on between meal times, so they are put on a 10-week healthy eating regimen to help them shift the pounds. Plus, office workers test the theory that by putting sweet treats even slightly out of sight, they can be kept out of mind

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The team face a challenging landing in the courtyard of a beautiful 18th-century building. An elderly woman needs treatment after being hit by a bus

programme.title: My Feet Are Killing Me
programme.desc: Dr Vincent performs a delicate surgery when she treats a toddler born with 12 toes. Plus, things get tense when a patient wakes up in the middle of their operation

programme.title: Embarrassing Bodies
programme.desc: Pixie McKenna investigates new surgery techniques involving the anus, and helps a man suffering with hot testicles. Dawn Harper meets a woman who had a stroke at just 45, revealing whether electrotherapy can help her regain more of her bodily functions, and investigates the worrying trend of buying legal highs online. Christian Jessen is joined by Nurse Joy Tickle to help treat assorted skin conditions for visitors in Birmingham, and in Magaluf there's a look at the serious side effects of spiking someone's drink

programme.title: Body Cam
programme.desc: Police officers find themselves pitted against a gunman holed up in a suburban home. Plus, the nerve of a rookie cop is tested when a routine stop on the highway goes wrong

programme.title: Ambulance Australia
programme.desc: The Queensland Ambulance Service are called to the scene of a horrific car crash. Paramedics Mat and Patia help a man who is anxious after taking drugs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton begin the fifth and final leg of their road trip in Wales in search of bargains, and it's all to play for as they head for an auction showdown in Newport, Shropshire

programme.title: The Inspectors Are Coming
programme.desc: Cameras follow UK law enforcement teams, beginning in Cleethorpes where beach inspectors struggle with their new powers to fine as it goes against their usual laid back style. In Dumfries and Galloway, experienced law enforcers Erin and Jim are dishing out £80 penalties to anyone caught dropping a cigarette butt. Meanwhile, in the London borough of Newham, John and Terry face one of their busiest nights ever - could they be about to beat their record for the most number of noise complaints?

programme.title: The Inspectors Are Coming
programme.desc: Two volatile roofers cause problems for an Inspector and she eventually has to call for police back up. Meanwhile in Cleethorpes, could a man who has got his car stuck on the beach be facing a £20,000 fine?

Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties for their £160,000 budget in and around the city of Fort Lauderdale

programme.desc: Anna Richardson meets Hull couple Dawn and Mike, along with their lodger Gareth, who are all puzzled as to why they are putting on weight. Twenty-four hours of CCTV footage showing their eating habits soon gives them all the answers they need, and they begin a 10-week healthy eating regime. A team of volunteers embarks on an experiment to test the theory that a smaller plate size can fool people into feeling full, and there is a look at the difference between what the food industry tells consumers and sells them

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s, telling the history of folk and protest music. Featuring interviews and footage of artists including Joan Baez, Woody Guthrie and Leonard Cohen

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Record producer Sam Phillips recalls how he discovered Elvis Presley. Landmark series from the 1970s, recounting the birth of rock 'n' roll

programme.title: The Flood (1963)
programme.desc: Eight children are trapped in a farmhouse by rising flood waters, but work together to find ways to survive until rescued. Drama, starring Waveney Lee and Ian Ellis

programme.title: Dear Murderer (1947)
programme.desc: A man believes he has committed the perfect crime when he kills his wife's two lovers in a jealous rage. Melodrama, starring Eric Portman, Dennis Price, Jack Warner, Greta Gynt and Hazel Court

programme.title: Far from the Madding Crowd (1967)
programme.desc: A beautiful woman arouses the ardour of three rival suitors in 19th-century rural Dorset and, having flirted and toyed with their affections, she chooses to marry a caddish, gambling soldier - a decision that leads to a sequence of harrowing events. John Schlesinger's adaptation of Thomas Hardy's classic novel, with Julie Christie, Alan Bates, Terence Stamp and Peter Finch

programme.title: The Captain's Table (1959)
programme.desc: A gruff cargo skipper is given the command of a luxurious liner, but finds it difficult to mind his manners among the upper-class passengers. Comedy, with John Gregson, Donald Sinden, Peggy Cummins and Reginald Beckwith

programme.title: The Man From Nowhere (1975)
programme.desc: A Victorian orphan is sent to live with her uncle, and is troubled by a ghostly presence that haunts his house. Supernatural mystery, starring Sarah Hollis Andrews and Ronald Adam

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: Author and entrepreneur Michael Hayman interviews the business leaders at the heart of London's most exciting success stories

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Sail the Saronic and Ionian Greek islands with Peter Maneas as he explores their rich history, culture, cuisine, and people as well as their hidden secrets and surprises

programme.title: Next Big Thing
programme.desc: International Arts Judge James Nicholls is searching for emerging painters, sculptors, photographers and street artists to help them develop and give them the chance to be the 'Next Big Thing'

programme.title: A Dozen Summers ()
programme.desc: Maisie and Daisy McCormack hijack a children's film and take the audience on a tour of their journey to adolescence

programme.title: Nothing But the Truth (2008)
programme.desc: A reporter uncovers an international political scandal, but breaking the story entails revealing the identity of an undercover CIA agent. She refuses to reveal her source to the government, and is imprisoned. While she takes her legal battle to the supreme court, the exposed spy finds her career on the line and her life in danger. Thriller, starring Kate Beckinsale and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: The Conversations (2016)
programme.desc: A disillusioned dentist tries to become a stand-up comic, but finds he is unable to perform on stage due to his unrequited love for an American woman who is about to return to the States, never to be seen again. In desperation he seeks a way to have her permanently erased from his memory. Comedy drama, starring Haider Zafar and Daniella Down

programme.title: Relentless (2020)
programme.desc: An ex-convict must stay out of trouble for one more day to secure his freedom - but must choose between his principles and his future when he comes to the aid of a prostitute under threat from a gang. Action thriller, starring Steven Murphy and Tiffany-Ellen Robinson

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael tries to get Joe Pesci to star in his proposed new film Bongo in the Congo, but receives an unenthusiastic response. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lonely residents of the street find company with each other as Christmas arrives. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Face at the Window (1939)
programme.desc: In 1880, Paris is terrorised by a hideous murderer. An innocent man is accused of being the killer by a respected aristocrat who longs to get him out of the way so he can seduce his fiancee - but may also have other reasons for wanting a scapegoat. Horror, starring Tod Slaughter and John Warwick

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal

programme.title: The Queen's Cavalry
programme.desc: The trainee troopers prepare for their final riding exam over one of the Army's toughest courses. Originally hopeless horseman Glenn is apprehensive - as is Adrian Van Loon, whose kit often fails to meet the rigorous standards. Gunners from the armoured regiment practise on the firing range with live ammunition, and the Household Cavalry's historian talks about their role in the battle of Waterloo

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls take on a new bodyguard while competing in a musical contest - but trouble ensues when all three of them fall in love with him

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: Kylie disappears and sends Kim and Kate a message saying she is quitting the band. The girls recruit a fan to take her place at a concert - unaware that she is not what she seems

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Animated fantasy adventures

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: BabaYaga offers to magically turn Jack into a great singer - but the spell goes wrong and he ends up swapping voices with a pig

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The Warlord puts Jack and Tashi in charge of the town for one day, because he knows it is about to come under attack

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures

programme.desc: The story of Operation Jericho, a 1944 mission that saw a squadron of RAF Mosquito bombers try to free French Resistance fighters from a prison in Amiens

programme.title: The Lady with the Lamp (1951)
programme.desc: Biopic of Florence Nightingale, who went down in medical history for her heroic efforts to treat thousands of casualties during the Crimean War, while the British Government belittled her for being a woman. Starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding

programme.title: Passport to Pimlico (1949)
programme.desc: The residents of a London suburb discover documents proving their neighbourhood is not part of Britain, and declare independence. Appointing their own head of state, they begin erecting borders as the government resorts to desperate measures to bring the community back under British rule. Ealing comedy, starring Stanley Holloway and Margaret Rutherford

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Abbey Wood, Ladywell. Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: Best of The Tube

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: The landmark series from the 1970s tells the story of the Beatles, featuring contributions by John Lennon and George Harrison

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens, which house a diverse collection of plants and are a leading centre for scientific research and conservation

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hardware

programme.title: I Train My Hands for War
programme.desc: Documentary about a boxing promoter preparing for his first cage fight at the age of 55. After training for 18 months he faces a fighter half his age

programme.title: Sophie's Choice (1982)
programme.desc: A Polish Holocaust survivor tries to start afresh in 1940s New York, but is tormented by ghosts from her nightmarish past, leaving her desperately seeking solace in a stormy relationship with an unpredictable chemist. But all is not as it seems, as a new neighbour seems set to discover. Drama, starring an Oscar-winning Meryl Streep alongside Kevin Kline and Peter MacNicol

programme.title: Elsa Fraulein SS (1977)
programme.desc: A sadistic Nazi prison guard oversees a train full of sex slaves, who are to be transported to the front lines. Asd she adopts brutal measures to keep her charges in submission, the French resistance plot to set the women free. Thriller, starring Malisa Longo and Olivier Mathot

programme.desc: Mick's preaching starts to annoy the neighbours and Dirk Bogarde reveals some information to the police. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell
